content.health_check: If today > last_verified_date + 180 days, emit STANDARD_STALE warning.
content.name: AIAP Quality Standard — Core v1.0.0

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {intent, confidence, route, state, op}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.description: Core syntax reference and quality checklist for AISOP files governed by AIAP protocol. Loadable extensions (security, ecosystem, performance) provide additional checks. Includes tool_annotations governance spec, I12 Tool Annotation Consistency rule, MF15 Output Contract Enforcement rule, json_schema for data contracts (JSON Schema Draft 2020-12), cache_policy dynamic TTL contract, lint_report data contract for automated verification, test_report with automated/subjective split, and license_spec (SPDX license field specification). Not an executable flow.
content.fractal_exempt: Standard library reference module — no executable flow graph.

params.input_variable: string|null, default: null, Extract from user message.

governance_params.description: AIAP governance parameters for all compliant AISOP files.

template.protocol: AIAP V1.0.0 — must match AIAP.md protocol field

governance_params.enforcement: Every .aisop.json file SHOULD include protocol field matching AIAP.md. AIAP.md MUST exist in project root.

trust_framework.description: Four-level trust classification for AIAP programs. Determines permission boundaries and validation requirements.

T1.name: Metadata Only
T1.permissions: Read AIAP.md metadata only. No tool access, no execution.

T2.name: Read-Only Sandbox
T2.permissions: Read-only tool access (file_system read). No write side_effects.

T3.name: Supervised Execution
T3.permissions: Read/write tools with explicit scope constraints. Human approval for destructive operations.
T3.validation: Full ThreeDimTest + I8 threat model + governance_hash required.

T4.name: Autonomous
T4.permissions: Full tool access within declared permissions scope. No per-operation human approval required.
T4.validation: Full ThreeDimTest + I8 + I9 + governance_hash + quality_baseline history.

trust_framework.default: T3 — programs default to supervised unless explicitly elevated. Per AIAP Protocol §16.3.
trust_framework.escalation: If runtime behavior exceeds declared trust_level (e.g., T2 program attempts write), executor MUST block and escalate.
trust_framework.aiap_md_format: trust_level field in AIAP.md: integer (1-4) for backward compatibility. Structured format { level: number, justification: string, constraints: [string] } is RECOMMENDED for T3+.

tool_annotations.description: Structured annotations for declared tools enabling safety analysis and interoperability mapping.

tool_schema.name: string — tool identifier matching tools[] entry
tool_schema.required: boolean — program cannot function without this tool

annotations.read_only: boolean — tool performs only read operations
annotations.destructive: boolean — tool can irreversibly modify/delete state
annotations.idempotent: boolean — repeated invocation produces same result

tool_annotations.enforcement: I12 verifies annotations match side_effects. ThreeDimTest I12 mandatory for Standard/Professional tier.
tool_annotations.backward_compatibility: Legacy tools[] without annotations remain valid — annotations default to null, I12 is N/A.

mcp_tool_mapping.description: Bidirectional mapping between AIAP tools[] and MCP Tools. Enables AIAP programs to be exposed as MCP Servers and consume external MCP Servers.

aiap_to_mcp.governance_extension: MCP Tool gains AIAP extension: { aiap_protocol, trust_level, governance_hash }

mcp_to_aiap.trust_policy: External MCP Servers inherit program trust_level constraints. REMOTE_UNVERIFIED MCP Servers capped at T2.

mcp_server_field.description: Optional mcp_server field in AIAP.md tools[] entries. Links an AIAP tool declaration to an external MCP Server.
mcp_server_field.schema: { server_id: string (MCP Server URI), tool_name: string (specific tool in server), transport: string (stdio|streamable-http) }

mcp_tool_mapping.backward_compatibility: Programs without mcp_server fields in tools[] operate identically to v14.0.0. MCP mapping is opt-in.

tool_dirs_spec.description: Specification for embedded tool directories in Pattern G programs. Programs declare tool_dirs in AIAP.md to bundle tool implementations.

common_rules.readme_required: Each tool directory MUST contain README.md documenting: purpose, interface (CLI args or JSON stdin/stdout), security constraints.
common_rules.network_declaration: If code contains network API calls, AIAP.md permissions.network.allowed must be true. Detected via I13(b) language-specific static analysis.
common_rules.write_scope: Code write operations must not exceed AIAP.md permissions.file_system.scope. Detected via I13(c).
common_rules.mcp_proxy: All code tools MUST be exposed via mcp_tools/mcp_server.json. Direct subprocess execution prohibited (shell_tools exempt with T4 audit).
common_rules.dependency_locking: All language-specific dependency files MUST use exact version pinning. I13(d) enforces per-language rules.

code_trust_gate.description: Security verification flow for bundled code before execution.
steps: 2. Static analysis: import declarations vs permissions.network
3. File hash verification: mcp_server.json file_hashes vs actual file SHA-256
4. Trust level check: Pattern G minimum T3

trust_modes.T3_supervised: Human confirmation required before MCP Server startup
trust_modes.T4_autonomous: Automatic pass after all checks succeed

tool_dirs_spec.governance_hash_coverage: governance_hash algorithm unchanged (SHA-256 of .aisop.json files). tool_dirs/ file hashes are recorded in mcp_server.json file_hashes field, which is part of mcp_tools/ and covered by governance_hash indirectly via .aisop.json referencing. Programs without tool_dirs have identical hash computation.
tool_dirs_spec.backward_compatibility: tool_dirs is purely optional. Programs without tool_dirs operate identically to v14. I13 and MF16 are N/A for non-Pattern-G programs.

instruction_invariant.rule: Every AISOP file's instruction field MUST be exactly: ASSERT RUN aisop.main
instruction_invariant.rationale: instruction is a machine execution directive using assertion semantics. ASSERT ensures the program MUST be executed — there is no option to skip or partially run. RUN is a command keyword. aisop.main is the structural path to the primary execution graph. Like Python's assert statement, ASSERT RUN means: execute this program; failure to execute is a fatal violation.
instruction_invariant.enforcement: ValidateStep checks instruction === 'ASSERT RUN aisop.main'. Any other value is a structural violation.

system_prompt_rules.description: system_prompt is the behavioral layer — defines HOW the agent should act, not WHAT it is or HOW it is built.
must_contain: Role positioning — behavioral role, not product name (e.g., 'Personal expense tracking assistant', not 'Expense Tracker v1.0.0')
Domain behavioral guidelines — domain-specific action principles (e.g., 'Prioritize numerical precision', 'Enforce quality standards rigorously')
'Mirror User's exact language and script variant.' — D6 multilingual requirement
'Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.' — Axiom 0 seal
must_not_contain: Product name or version — redundant with name + version fields
Architecture or pattern details — redundant with description field
Module file names or delegation logic — redundant with functions field
Capability lists — redundant with summary field
system_prompt_rules.template: {Behavioral role}. {Domain guidelines}. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.\nAlign Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.
system_prompt_rules.principle: Each field has exactly one responsibility. Information appears in exactly one place.
system_prompt_rules.enforcement: ThreeDimTest D6 verifies multilingual + Align presence. ValidateStep checks no redundancy with name/version/description.

content.summary: Short description of what this AISOP does.
content.system_prompt:
AISOP quality standard and syntax reference. Enforce AIAP governance invariants rigorously. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.

C1.name: Problem Modeling
C1.check: Mermaid graph matches domain archetype: Pipeline/Router/Loop/Hybrid. EDGE COMPLETENESS: every conditional path described in function logic must have a corresponding edge in the Mermaid graph.
C1.how_to_verify: Draw Mermaid. Verify it matches one of four canonical shapes. EDGE COMPLETENESS: for each function, compare described conditional paths (e.g., 'if empty → route to Present', 'if NoData → skip to X') against Mermaid edges. Every described path must have a corresponding edge. Use conditional labels for alternate paths (e.g., 'LoadData -- HasData --> ModeGate' and 'LoadData -- NoData --> Present'). Missing edges are C1 violations.

C2.name: Node Load
C2.check: Leaf function <=8 steps. Convergence function <=12 steps.
C2.how_to_verify: Count step# keys in each function. Flag any >8 (leaf) or >12 (convergence).

C3.name: Abstraction Consistency
C3.check: All Mermaid nodes at similar abstraction level.
C3.how_to_verify: Read all node labels. They should be ~2-4 words, same grammatical form.

C4.name: Information Flow
C4.check: Every parameter referenced in >=1 function. All outputs consumed downstream.
C4.how_to_verify: List parameters. Grep each in functions. List outputs. Grep each downstream.

C5.name: Fractal Decomposition
C5.check: Target AIAP fractal structure per AIAP_Protocol.md. Nodes match functionality.
C5.how_to_verify: Count functional nodes (total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode). Apply progressive thresholds. Check Pattern selection. Verify main quality constraint.
C5.functional_node_count: Functional nodes = total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode. Start and endNode are structural overhead, not functionality.

progressive_thresholds.advisory: 13-15 functional nodes — WARNING: suggest review for extraction opportunities. Provide specific split suggestions based on tool boundaries, data flow phases, and independence test.
progressive_thresholds.recommended: 16+ functional nodes — MANDATORY sub_mermaid feasibility check before accepting fractal_exempt (B8). ThreeDimTest must analyze Mermaid graph for: (1) independent sub-graphs with single entry/exit, (2) 3+ repeated structural patterns, (3) clear functional phase boundaries. If decomposable sub-graphs found → RED: must decompose. If no decomposable sub-graphs (true orchestrator) → YELLOW accepted with justification. fractal_exempt alone is insufficient — feasibility check is non-negotiable.
progressive_thresholds.decomposition_output: When B8 feasibility analysis finds decomposable sub-graphs, ThreeDimTest MUST generate a concrete sub_mermaid_proposal containing: (1) decomposition pattern identified (FAN_OUT: node with 3+ mutually exclusive branches converging to same target; SEQUENTIAL: linear chain of 8+ nodes with phase boundaries; CONDITIONAL: decision node with 2-3 substantial branches), (2) new main Mermaid string with SubRef[aisop.<name>] replacing extracted nodes, (3) new sub_mermaid Mermaid string with Entry routing node + extracted handler nodes + SubEnd, (4) delegate function for SubRef node, (5) entry/routing function for sub-graph entry, (6) fractal_exempt text with node distribution and execution mode. The proposal is attached to test_report as RED_WITH_FIX. Detection without a concrete proposal is INCOMPLETE — B8 check FAILS if it identifies a decomposable graph but does not provide the decomposition. Verification: |V_main| <= 15 AND |V_sub| <= 15.

C5.fractal_exempt: Module author may add fractal_exempt field in system.content with reason string to suppress progressive warnings. Equivalent to Python # noqa.

main_quality_constraint.pattern_a: main is the only file — contains all logic, no special constraint.
main_quality_constraint.pattern_b_plus: main is router — only routing logic + lightweight inline handlers (≤2 steps, no tool calls). No business logic. tools = union of all sub-module tools. Route count determined by functionality, no node limit.

module_rules.self_contained: Each module: independent system_prompt, tools, parameters, functions.
module_rules.minimum: ≥3 functional nodes + (≥1 tool call OR ≥3 steps).

pattern_thresholds.A: Single file, 1 function — no hard node limit
pattern_thresholds.B: Multi-file, 2+ independent functions, main=router + func modules
pattern_thresholds.C: Package + shared.aisop.json when 2+ modules reuse complex logic
pattern_thresholds.D: Nested (max 2 levels) when sub-module has 2+ sub-functions
pattern_thresholds.F: Ecosystem: 3+ AISOP components + blueprint + shared data_contracts
pattern_thresholds.G: Embedded Runtime: Pattern E/F + tool_dirs/ directory with bundled tool implementations (python_tools/, mcp_tools/, etc.)

C5.independence_test: Can describe in one sentence + no shared specialized tools/state + can test independently -> separate file.

split_priority.P0_sub_mermaid: In-file sub-graph decomposition. Preferred when nodes share parameters/context, sub-graphs have data dependencies, or splitting would duplicate shared logic. Apply before considering file-level split. Per Protocol §7.1.
split_priority.P1_tool_boundary: Different node groups use different tool sets -> natural split point.
split_priority.P2_data_flow_phase: Sequential pipeline phases (research → generate → test) -> each phase is split candidate.
split_priority.no_split: Convergence groups (fan-in), error recovery loops, tightly coupled same-tool nodes -> keep together.

C5.enforcement: Generate checks functional node count. 2+ independent functions -> suggest Pattern B. Progressive warnings at 13+ and 16+. Never FAIL on node count alone.
C5.tool_weight_factor: TOOL WEIGHT FACTOR FOR FRACTAL THRESHOLD: zero-tool modules (modules with empty tools[] or no tools declared) have lower per-node cognitive complexity because nodes contain pure text instructions without tool invocation logic. For zero-tool modules: ADVISORY threshold raised to 15 functional nodes (from 13), RECOMMENDED SPLIT threshold raised to 18 (from 16). For modules with file_system write operations: standard thresholds apply (13 ADVISORY, 16 RECOMMENDED). For modules with network tools (web_search, web_fetch): standard thresholds apply. Rationale: tool-invoking nodes require parameter marshaling, error handling, and response parsing — approximately 1.5x cognitive load per node compared to pure-logic nodes.

sub_mermaid_decomposition.description: sub_mermaid is in-file decomposition using multiple Mermaid graphs within a single .aisop.json. Preferred over file-level splitting when functional cohesion is high.

structure_rules.entry_point: Always ASSERT RUN aisop.main. Main graph references sub-graphs via NodeName[aisop.sub_name] syntax.
structure_rules.functions: Single flat functions dictionary shared across all sub-graphs.
structure_rules.params: Defined once at root level, shared across all sub-graphs.
structure_rules.sub_graph_structure: Each sub-graph has its own Start node and End node (named {SubGraphName}Start and {SubGraphName}End or standard Start/endNode).

node_counting.total_functional: Sum of (graph nodes − Start − endNode) across all sub-graphs.
node_counting.single_path_max: Count main + sum(all sub-graphs) under worst-case single invocation path.
node_counting.threshold_target: Apply §6 progressive thresholds to single_path_max, not total_functional.
node_counting.fractal_exempt_required: If single_path_max > 16, fractal_exempt annotation is required.

node_count_convention.functional_node: A key in the functions{} dict of the .aisop.json file
node_count_convention.includes: Start with function definition counts as functional
node_count_convention.excludes: endNode never counts, bare Start without function never counts

when_to_use.prefer_sub_mermaid: (a) Nodes share parameters and working context, (b) sub-graphs have data dependencies, (c) splitting would duplicate shared logic.
when_to_use.prefer_file_split: (a) Sub-graphs use different tool sets, (b) sub-graphs are independently testable, (c) single sub-graph still exceeds 16 nodes after decomposition.

function_body_complexity.description: Second dimension of complexity measurement — complements node counting by detecting high-density functions where a single node contains excessive logic.

metrics.steps_per_function: Count of step keys (step1, step2, ..., stepN) in a single function.
metrics.directives_per_step: Count of named directive blocks within a step, identified by UPPERCASE LABEL: pattern (e.g., 'TOOL ANNOTATIONS:', 'INCREMENTAL GENERATION:').

thresholds.steps_advisory: 8+ steps in one function → ADVISORY: consider sub_mermaid decomposition to distribute steps across sub-graph functions.
thresholds.directives_advisory: 15+ directives in one step → ADVISORY: consider splitting the step into multiple functions via sub_mermaid.
thresholds.directives_recommended: 20+ directives in one step → RECOMMENDED: decompose via sub_mermaid or file split. Document in DECOMPOSITION PLAN if retaining.

function_body_complexity.interaction_with_node_count: Function body complexity check runs independently of node count check. A file can trigger advisory from either dimension. Both dimensions must be addressed.

C6.name: Brain Principle
C6.check: LLM-native (reasoning): no tool needed. Tool-required (file I/O, API): declare tool.
C6.how_to_verify: Review each function. File/API calls -> must be in tools[]. Classify/summarize -> no tool needed.

C7.name: Token Budget Compliance
C7.check: Module system_prompt or description declares token_estimate range. AIAP.md runtime.token_budget is set.
C7.how_to_verify: Check AIAP.md runtime.token_budget exists and is >0. For each module, verify description or system_prompt mentions expected complexity or token range. Standalone modules may use simplified estimate.

I1.name: Tool Honesty
I1.check: No phantom (declared unused) and no undeclared (used but not declared). EXEMPTION: Pattern B+ main router files where tools = union of sub-module tools (per C5) — sub-module tools in main's tools[] are not phantom.
I1.how_to_verify: tools[] list. Grep each in functions. Grep tool verbs in functions, verify declared.

I2.name: Security
I2.check: Start.constraints: type guard, injection guard, size guard, encoding guard — always required. Path guard — required only when file_system in tools[]. Without file_system, 4 guards suffice. ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION: for tools=[] systems, (C) Injection guard should focus on prompt injection (role override, system prompt extraction, persona manipulation, 'ignore previous instructions') rather than shell metacharacters — shell injection is irrelevant without shell/file_system tools.
I2.how_to_verify: Read Start.constraints. Check tools[] for file_system. If present: verify all 5 guards. If absent: verify 4 guards (type, injection, size, encoding).
I2.inheritance_rule: Pattern B+ sub-modules: if main router's Start.constraints implements all 5 guards (A-E) and sub-module is ONLY accessible via main router (not independently invocable), sub-module may declare inherited guards with explicit '(inherited from main router)' annotation. ThreeDimTest treats annotated inherited guards as PASS. Sub-module MUST still implement (A) Type guard and (D) Path guard independently — these require module-specific knowledge. Inheritable guards: (B) Size, (C) Injection, (E) Encoding.
I2.design_note: Inheritance is OPTIONAL — programs may choose defense-in-depth by implementing full guards in every sub-module independently. The AIAP Creator itself uses this approach: all sub-modules have full I2 guards despite being eligible for inheritance from main. Both approaches are valid; inheritance reduces redundancy while full guards maximize defense depth.

I3.name: Data Integrity
I3.check: Persistent writes: atomic (.tmp then rename), backup (.bak), field-level schema. EXEMPTION: if tools=[] (no file_system, no shell, no network), I3 is automatically N/A — no persistent writes are possible without tool access.
I3.how_to_verify: First check tools[]: if empty, mark I3 as N/A (5/5). Otherwise, find all write operations. Verify 'atomic'/'tmp'/'rename' and 'bak'/'backup' mentioned.

I4.name: Circuit Breaker
I4.check: Error: retry -> circuit-breaker -> named-fallback -> inform. Fallback must specify concrete action. EXEMPTION: exact-match routing gates (IntentGate, ModeGate, TypeGate) with a deterministic default case may use simplified Error: 'Unknown {param} -> fallback: default {route} -> inform.' (no retry/circuit-breaker needed since routing cannot fail with a default).
I4.how_to_verify: Read Error fields. Pattern: retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: {specific action} -> inform.

I5.name: Tool Suitability
I5.check: Tool matches Brain Principle C6. Utilize tools >30% of functions. EXEMPTION 1 (Symbolic): modules that explicitly declare read-only symbolic analysis (no actual tool execution) are exempt — their tool usage is inherently low by design. EXEMPTION 2 (Delegation): Pattern B+ orchestrators/advisors where >=50% of functions are delegation wrappers or LLM-native advisory functions are exempt — verify that delegated sub-module individually meet the 30% threshold instead. EXEMPTION 3 (Zero-tool): if tools=[] (empty array) and no function in the module references any tool call, the module is automatically exempt — zero-tool systems are by definition pure LLM-native and I5 is not applicable. AUTO-RECOGNITION: modules matching the 'read-once-analyze' pattern are automatically eligible for Symbolic exemption without explicit annotation.
I5.how_to_verify: Count functions calling tools. Divide by total. >30% -> OK. EXEMPTION 1: If module declares 'symbolic'/'no actual tool execution'/'read-only analysis', apply exemption. EXEMPTION 2: If module is Pattern B+ with >=50% delegation/advisory functions (identified by 'Delegate to X.aisop.json' or LLM-native reasoning steps), exempt the module and verify each delegated sub-module meets 30% independently. EXEMPTION 3 (Zero-tool): if tools=[] and no function references any tool call, auto-exempt — I5 is N/A for zero-tool systems. This pattern is common in conversational, advisory, and classification-only modules. AUTO-RECOGNITION (read-once-analyze): if exactly 1 function calls a tool for data loading (LoadData, ReadData, FetchData or similar name pattern) and ALL remaining functions perform no tool calls (pure LLM-native analysis/formatting/presentation), auto-apply Symbolic exemption — no explicit annotation required. This pattern is common in search, statistics, and analytics modules.

I6.name: Resilience + Semantic Accuracy
I6.check: Every function has Error field with correct pattern. Error/fallback text must be semantically accurate — hardcoded counts must match actual content.
I6.how_to_verify: STRUCTURAL: count functions, each must have Error field with retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform pattern. SEMANTIC: extract numbers from Error/fallback text (e.g., 'list all N features', 'present N options'), cross-reference against actual list lengths in same function's steps. Mismatch is FAIL.

I7.name: Parameter Completeness + Metadata Consistency
I7.check: All parameters: type + agentic_prompt. Metadata fields internally consistent: name embedded version must match version field, id module-name must match filename.
I7.how_to_verify: PARAMETERS: list all, each must have 'type' and 'agentic_prompt', each must be referenced in at least one function. METADATA: if name contains vX.Y.Z pattern, extract and compare against version field — mismatch is FAIL. Verify id's module suffix matches filename stem (e.g., id 'foo.main' → file 'main.aisop.json').

I12.name: Tool Annotation Consistency
I12.check: I12 TOOL ANNOTATION CONSISTENCY — PER-MODULE CHECK: for single-module programs (Pattern A): verify program-level tool annotations match module side_effects. For multi-module programs (Pattern B+): check at MODULE granularity — for each module, verify only the tools that module actually uses (listed in its tools[] array) have annotations consistent with that module's side_effects[]. ZERO-TOOL MODULE EXEMPTION: modules with no tools declared (tools[] empty or absent, e.g., safety modules operating on pure input text) are automatically N/A for I12 — exclude from scoring denominator per dynamic_weights.na_handling. This prevents false positives where program-level tool declarations (e.g., file_system: read_only=false) contradict a module that simply doesn't use that tool.
I12.how_to_verify: For each tool in AIAP.md tools[]: (1) if annotations.read_only=true, verify tool NOT in any module's write side_effects. (2) if annotations.destructive=true, verify trust_level >= T3 AND module has explicit confirmation step. (3) if annotations.open_world=true, verify I8 addresses AT3. (4) if annotations.idempotent=true, verify module declares idempotent=true.
I12.na_conditions: Programs without annotations in tools[] entries. Legacy format programs are I12 N/A. ZERO-TOOL MODULE EXEMPTION: modules with no tools declared (tools[] empty or absent) are automatically N/A for I12.
I12.relationship: Extends I1 (Tool Honesty): I1 checks existence, I12 checks declared behavior matches actual behavior.

I13.name: Embedded Code Safety
I13.check: Bundled code in tool_dirs/ passes integrity, network declaration, file scope, dependency locking, MCP proxy, shell audit, trust ceiling, permission subset, recursion depth, and governance hash checks.
I13.how_to_verify: (a) Code integrity — all source files in tool_dirs/ have SHA-256 hashes recorded in mcp_tools/mcp_server.json file_hashes field, governance_hash covers mcp_server.json. (b) Network declaration — detect language-specific network API imports (Python: requests/urllib/httpx/aiohttp; TS/JS: fetch/axios/got; Go: net/http; Rust: reqwest/hyper; Shell: curl/wget/nc); if detected, permissions.network.allowed must be true. (c) File system scope — detect language-specific write operations; write paths must not exceed permissions.file_system.scope. (d) Dependency version locking — Python: requirements.txt must use == only (no >= or *); TS/JS: package.json must not use ^ or ~ prefixes, lock file must exist; Go: go.sum must exist; Rust: Cargo.lock must exist. (e) MCP proxy required — all code tool directories must be exposed via mcp_tools/mcp_server.json exposed_tools (shell_tools exempt with audit). (f) Shell audit — if shell_tools/ exists, trust_level must be >= T4 and AIAP.md must contain shell_audit_approved: true. (g) AIAP trust ceiling — when aiap_tools/ exists, for each sub-program directory, read AIAP.md trust_level; verify sub trust_level <= parent trust_level. If sub declares T4 but parent is T3, FAIL. (h) AIAP permission subset — when aiap_tools/ exists, for each sub-program, verify sub permissions.file_system.scope is within parent scope (path prefix match); verify sub permissions.network.endpoints is subset of parent endpoints. If sub declares endpoints not in parent, FAIL. (i) AIAP recursion depth — when aiap_tools/ exists, traverse sub-program directories recursively; count nesting depth of aiap_tools/ within aiap_tools/; if depth > 3, FAIL. (j) AIAP governance hash — when aiap_tools/ exists, for each sub-program, recompute governance_hash per PL14 and compare to sub-program's AIAP.md governance_hash field; if mismatch, FAIL.
I13.na_conditions: Programs without tool_dirs (Pattern A-F). I13 is N/A for non-Pattern-G programs.
I13.relationship: Extends I2 (Security Guards) to cover embedded code. Complements I9 (Supply Chain) for bundled dependencies.

I_PERSONA.name: System Prompt Personality Protection
I_PERSONA.description: During evolution, system_prompt text constituting personality/tone (sentences containing identity verbs, communication style directives, transparency declarations) is treated as personality_anchor. Generator MUST: (1) append-only — new capability descriptions added at end, (2) diff-verify — compare pre/post evolution system_prompt, reject modifications to personality_anchor segments, (3) user-confirm — if modification unavoidable, present side-by-side diff requiring explicit user approval.
I_PERSONA.applies_to: Evolve operations on programs with system_prompt

D1.name: Step Clarity
D1.check: Concrete action verbs: extract, classify, calculate, generate, read, write, validate. No vague 'process', 'handle', 'do'. All .aisop.json and AIAP.md files MUST be encoded as UTF-8 without BOM. JSON read/write operations MUST specify UTF-8 encoding explicitly, especially on platforms where system default encoding may differ (e.g., Windows with Shift-JIS or GBK).
D1.how_to_verify: Read all steps. Flag steps starting with 'process', 'handle', 'do', 'manage'.

D2.name: Step Format
D2.check: Tool steps: Input/Process/Output/Error. LLM steps: action + expected output.
D2.how_to_verify: Tool-calling steps must mention input source and output destination. Step keys should follow sequential integer naming (step1, step2, ... stepN); non-standard suffixes (step1_5, step2b, step4c) are permitted for insertion ordering but count as minor format inconsistency.

D3.name: Null Propagation
D3.check: Downstream handles null from Error nodes.
D3.how_to_verify: Trace error paths. Downstream nodes must handle null input.

D4.name: Consistency + Internal Coherence
D4.check: FORMAT: CamelCase Mermaid node IDs, snake_case parameter names. SEMANTIC: steps must not contradict own constraints. REFERENCE: any number mentioned in text (menu items, feature counts, module counts) must match actual count. CROSS-FUNCTION: if function A names function B in steps/constraints/Error text, B must exist in same file. CONSTRAINT CROSS-VALIDATION: constraints across different functions in the same module must not logically contradict each other.
D4.how_to_verify: FORMAT: scan node IDs (CamelCase), params (snake_case). SEMANTIC: compare step actions vs constraints per function. REFERENCE: extract numbers from step/Error text, cross-check against actual list lengths (e.g., 'present 9 options' → verify 9 options exist). CROSS-FUNCTION: collect all function names referenced in text, verify each exists as a key in functions object. CONSTRAINT CROSS-VALIDATION: (a) If any function declares 'Read-only' or equivalent restriction in constraints, no other function in the same module may perform contradicting operations (e.g., write/delete) in its steps. (b) If a routing function declares 'Exactly one route' or similar exclusivity constraint, the same function's steps/constraints must not also describe executing multiple routes simultaneously. (c) Mermaid edge count from a decision node must match the number of routes described in that node's function logic.

D5.name: Boundary Rules
D5.check: NLU/decision nodes have explicit disambiguation signals for confusable intent pairs. Non-NLU routing nodes (ModeGate, TypeGate) that route by exact parameter match do not require boundary rules.
D5.how_to_verify: Identify confusable pairs. Each must have a disambiguation SIGNAL keyword. Skip exact-match parameter routers (e.g., ModeGate routing by research_mode='structure'|'quality'|'compliance'). SUB-ROUTE CHECK: if NLU/router function defines sub-routes (per sub_routing_rules), verify each sub-route has: trigger patterns, target node, behavior mode, and edge-case fallback. Verify sub-route triggers do not overlap with primary intent signals without disambiguation.

D6.name: Multilingual
D6.check: system_prompt contains 'Mirror User's exact language and script variant.'
D6.how_to_verify: Grep system_prompt for 'Mirror User' or 'exact language and script variant'.

D7.name: Metabolism
D7.check: No phantom tools, no dead-end nodes, no vestigial steps.
D7.how_to_verify: List nodes with no outbound edges. List unused tools. List unreferenced functions.

pattern_e_validation.description: PATTERN E VALIDATION CHECKS (PE1-PE6): applicable when program pattern includes 'E' (Package with memory/ directory).

PE1.name: Memory Directory Structure
PE1.check: Verify that AIAP.md memory architecture declaration matches actual module file_system write paths. All declared subdirectories (e.g., working.json, episodic/, semantic/, profiles/) must have at least one module writing to them.

PE2.name: Write Path Cross-Module Consistency
PE2.check: All modules writing to memory/ must use consistent path templates (same directory structure, same filename conventions). Detect and flag path divergence (e.g., one module writes 'memory/semantic/{user_id}/data.json', another writes 'memory/semantic/data_{user_id}.json').

PE3.name: Read Coverage
PE3.check: For each file written by any module into memory/, verify that at least one module reads from that path. Orphan writes (data written but never read) flagged as YELLOW.

PE4.name: Schema Version Consistency
PE4.check: If any memory/ file uses schema_version field, all memory/ files should declare schema_version. Version mismatches across files flagged as YELLOW.

PE5.name: Backup Recovery Path Verification
PE5.check: If any module creates .bak backup files, verify that the recovery logic (reading .bak on corruption) is present in the corresponding load/read module. Backup without recovery path flagged as YELLOW.

PE6.name: Memory Write Authority Check
PE6.check: MEMORY WRITE AUTHORITY: Verify that only modules explicitly designated as memory writers have file_system write access to the memory/ directory. Check: (1) Identify all modules with file_system tool declared AND side_effects containing 'file_write'. (2) If a module that was previously zero-tool or idempotent:true now has file_system write, flag as WARN: 'Module {name} gained memory/ write access — verify write scope is limited to a specific subdirectory (e.g., memory/safety_audit/) and file locking protocol is documented.' (3) Recommended: each memory-writing module should document its write scope as a constraint. Score: 5.0 if all writers are documented, 3.5 if undocumented writer detected.

dynamic_weights.description: Tier-based weight adjustments for ThreeDimTest weighted_score calculation.

Simple.rationale: Simple programs have fewer intrinsic concerns (no multi-file, minimal tools), so Cognitive and Intrinsic are weighted equally.

Standard.rationale: Default weights — Intrinsic quality (security, resilience) most important for production programs.

Professional.rationale: Same as Standard — Professional tier raises severity levels, not weight distribution.

dynamic_weights.na_handling: When I8, I9, I10, I11, I12, and/or I13 are N/A (e.g., non-published programs, no external dependencies, no trust_level declared, no identity block, no tool annotations), exclude them from the Intrinsic dimension denominator. Calculate I score as sum(applicable_scores) / count(applicable_items). This prevents unfair penalization for items that are structurally inapplicable. DENOMINATOR NORMALIZATION FOR REGRESSION CHECKS: when comparing scores across versions (QRG1-QRG5), if the set of N/A items differs between versions (denominator change due to items transitioning from N/A to applicable), the comparison MUST normalize the old score to the new denominator before computing deltas. Compute normalized_old_score by assuming new items would have scored the mean of existing applicable items. Use normalized score for regression detection to prevent false warnings when adopting new STANDARD features.
dynamic_weights.enforcement: ThreeDimTest MUST select weights based on tier classification. Generate ThreeDimTest step5 implements this logic.
dynamic_weights.I11_delegation_support: DELEGATION PATTERN: Score 5.0 if: (a) Module implements own rate limiting, OR (b) Module explicitly documents rate limiting delegation to another module with a named delegation note (e.g., 'RATE LIMITING DELEGATION: enforced by safety.aisop.json RateLimiter'). The delegatee module MUST actually implement rate limiting (verify cross-reference). Score 3.5 ONLY if rate limiting is absent AND no delegation documentation exists.
dynamic_weights.DELEGATION_PATTERN: For any ThreeDimTest item (I11, I12, etc.) where responsibility is intentionally delegated to another module in a multi-module program: (1) The delegating module must include a named DELEGATION note: '{ITEM}_DELEGATION: delegated to {module}.{node}'. (2) The delegatee module must actually implement the capability. (3) If both conditions met: score 5.0 (not 3.5). (4) If delegation claimed but delegatee doesn't implement: score 1.0 (worse than no delegation). This pattern applies to I11 (rate limiting), I9 (injection guards), I10 (output sanitization), and any other item where centralized implementation is architecturally valid.

tier_rules.description: Single source of truth for all tier-specific check adjustments. ThreeDimTest and Modify MUST reference these rules.

Simple.I2: WARNING (at least type guard required; 4 guards if no file_system; prompt-injection focus if tools=[] per STANDARD I2 ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION)
Simple.I3: skip if no file_system in tools[] (no persistent writes possible); N/A if tools=[] per STANDARD I3 EXEMPTION
Simple.I5: check exemptions: Symbolic, Delegation, Zero-tool, Auto-Recognition per STANDARD I5

Standard.I2: FAIL if no input validation (all applicable guards — 4 without file_system, 5 with file_system; prompt-injection focus if tools=[] per STANDARD I2 ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION)
Standard.I3: N/A if tools=[] per STANDARD I3 EXEMPTION; FAIL with file_system if no atomic writes
Standard.I5: check exemptions: Symbolic, Delegation, Zero-tool, Auto-Recognition per STANDARD I5

Professional.I3: FAIL if file_system present without atomic writes; N/A if tools=[]
Professional.D5: FAIL if boundary rules missing for confusable pairs

named_circuit_breaker_pattern.template: Error: {action} fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: {specific named alternative} -> inform user.
good_examples: fallback: list workspace dirs for manual selection
fallback: display patched JSON as copyable code block

upgrade_path.description: Grade Achievement priority by score_impact: I2 > I4 > I3 > I6 > D5 > C2 > I1 > D1 > D7.
upgrade_path.trigger: Any dimension grade below A -> run modify.aisop.json.

custom_fields.description: Source AISOP files may contain fields not defined in STANDARD (e.g. verified_on, custom metadata). Preserve unrecognized fields from source AISOP during evolution/modification — do NOT strip them. Custom fields are pass-through: copy as-is to output without validation.
custom_fields.semantic_validation: After evolution, if verified_on exists and content changed beyond cosmetic fixes, note in description that verification status is stale. Do NOT auto-remove or modify the verified_on array.

license_spec.description: Software license declaration for AIAP programs. Uses SPDX License Identifier standard.

conditional_fields.terms_url: string (URL) — required when license='proprietary', at least one of terms_url or contact must be present
conditional_fields.contact: string (email) — required when license='proprietary', at least one of terms_url or contact must be present

license_spec.store_requirement: Mandatory for all AIAP programs (§3.1 required field, per PL25). Default 'proprietary' when user does not specify.

copyright.description: Copyright notice for the AIAP program. Optional (§3.2).
copyright.format: Free-text copyright notice (e.g. 'Copyright 2026 AIXP Labs AIXP.dev')
copyright.store_behavior: Displayed in aiap-store listing if present.

author.format: Free-text (typically domain name or organization name)
author.store_behavior: Displayed in aiap-store listing if present.

description_hygiene.description: Prevent description and summary field bloat from accumulated version history.
description_hygiene.rule: If description or summary contains >2 version history entries (e.g. 'v1.1.0 adds... v1.2.0 adds... v1.3.0 adds...'), compress: keep current version changes in full, collapse older entries into 'Prior versions added: [feature1, feature2, ...].' Single summary line replaces multiple vX.Y.Z entries.
description_hygiene.summary_guideline: AIAP.md summary field should be a concise feature overview describing what the program does. Recommended ≤500 characters. Do not include version history — use .version_history/ directory and CHANGELOG.md for that purpose. D8-SUMMARY AUTO-CHECK (B1): ThreeDimTest D8 automatically checks summary length. If > 500 characters → YELLOW warning with actual character count. This is an automated enforcement of the ≤500 guideline.
description_hygiene.example_before: ... v1.1.0 adds substitution. v1.2.0 adds allergen notes. v1.3.0 adds nutrition.
description_hygiene.example_after: ... Prior versions added: substitution, allergen notes. v1.3.0 adds nutrition.

summary_consistency.description: Ensure summary field reflects current AISOP capabilities after evolution.
summary_consistency.rule: After evolution, if LEVEL_A or LEVEL_B changes added or removed features, verify summary still accurately describes the AISOP. If summary is stale (missing new key features or mentioning removed features), update summary to reflect current state. Summary should be 1-2 sentences, not a changelog.
summary_consistency.check: Compare summary against: (1) current tools[], (2) current Mermaid node names, (3) description's current-version section.

name_version_sync.description: Ensure name field's embedded version matches the version field after evolution.
name_version_sync.rule: If name field contains a version pattern (e.g., 'vX.Y.Z'), it MUST match the version field. After version bump, update all module name fields to reflect the new version. Newly created files get the correct version automatically; this rule targets existing files being evolved.
name_version_sync.check: Regex match vX.Y.Z in name field; compare against version field. Mismatch is a FAIL.

error_text_consistency.description: Prevent hardcoded counts and references in Error/fallback text from going stale after evolution.
error_text_consistency.rule: If an Error or fallback field contains a hardcoded count (e.g., 'list all N features', 'present N options'), that count MUST match the actual count in the corresponding function's step content. After adding or removing items (features, intents, menu options), scan all Error fields in the same file for stale counts.
error_text_consistency.check: Extract numbers from Error fields; cross-reference against actual list lengths in the same function's steps.

range_reference_consistency.description: Prevent stale item range references from persisting after checklist evolution.
range_reference_consistency.rule: If description text or AIAP.md body contains item range references (e.g., 'C1-C7', 'I1-I13', 'PL1-PL27'), the upper bound MUST match the actual item count in the STANDARD quality_checklist. Stale ranges (e.g., 'I1-I12' when STANDARD has I1-I13) MUST be updated when new items are added. Automated: D4-RANGE lint check in ThreeDimTest.

nlu_intent_checklist.description: When evolution adds or removes an NLU intent, all derived references must be updated. This checklist ensures completeness.
on_intent_add: (1) Add confusion pair(s) for the new intent vs most-similar existing intents with SIGNAL keywords
(2) Update tiebreaker order in NLU to include new intent at correct priority
(3) Update NLU enrichment steps if new intent requires additional context extraction
(4) Update Start menu item count and list to include new intent
(5) Add Mermaid edge from NLU to new intent's target node
(6) Update AIAP.md intent_examples with representative input for new intent
(7) Update AIAP.md modules[].nodes if new node was added
(8) Verify Error text counts in NLU and Start functions match new totals
on_intent_remove: (1) Remove confusion pairs referencing removed intent
(3) Remove Mermaid edge to removed intent's node
(4) Update Start menu count and list
(6) Update AIAP.md modules[].nodes if node was removed
(7) Verify no remaining functions reference removed intent by name
nlu_intent_checklist.enforcement: Generate EVOLUTION IMPACT SCAN must execute this checklist when evolution_plan contains NLU intent changes. ThreeDimTest D4+D5 provide last-line detection for stale counts and missing boundary rules.

derived_reference_rules.description: Ensure all derived references stay consistent after evolution. A derived reference is any text that restates or counts information defined elsewhere in the same file.

categories.count_references: Any number in Error/fallback/step text that refers to a list length (features, intents, menu items, modules). Must match actual count.
categories.version_references: Any version string embedded in name, description, or step text. Must match the version field.
categories.cross_function_references: Any function name mentioned in another function's steps/constraints/Error. Must exist as a key in the functions object.
categories.menu_references: Start/General menu item listings. Must include all routable intents defined in NLU/routing functions.

derived_reference_rules.enforcement: Generate MUST run EVOLUTION IMPACT SCAN to trace derived references after evolution. ApplyFixes MUST run DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT as final consistency check. ThreeDimTest I6+I7+D4 provide last-line detection.

intent_vector_guidance.description: Guidance for optional semantic routing vectors in AIAP programs with NLU routing.
intent_vector_guidance.when_to_use: Programs with 5+ NLU intents where keyword-based classification has known confusion pairs. Vector routing reduces ambiguity by comparing user input embedding against pre-computed intent embeddings.
intent_vector_guidance.generation_rule: For each NLU intent, select 3-5 representative input examples. Compute embeddings using a consistent model. Store as intent_vectors in AIAP.md. Include similarity_threshold (default 0.85).
intent_vector_guidance.hybrid_routing: Vector similarity is advisory — final routing decision combines: (1) vector similarity score, (2) keyword SIGNAL matches from NLU confusion pairs, (3) LLM judgment. Vector match > threshold → boost confidence by 20%. No match → fall through to keyword+LLM.
intent_vector_guidance.maintenance: Re-generate vectors when NLU intents change (add/remove). Stale vectors (generated before intent changes) should be flagged by ThreeDimTest D4.
intent_vector_guidance.constraints: Vectors are OPTIONAL. Programs without intent_vectors use existing keyword+LLM routing with no degradation. Embedding model dependency must be declared in tools[] if used at runtime.

protocol_alignment_rules.description: Rules for automatic protocol alignment checking within the Creator pipeline. ProtocolAlign runs between Research1 and EvolveStep.
trigger_conditions: STANDARD files updated since last pipeline run
alignment_checks: MCP tool manifest consistency — declared tools match actual usage
A2A Agent Card field completeness — skills[], capabilities, authentication present
SKILL.md frontmatter validity — required fields present and well-formed
protocol_alignment_rules.enforcement: Advisory — ProtocolAlign identifies gaps and delegates to advisor for remediation suggestions. Does not block pipeline execution.
protocol_alignment_rules.pipeline_position: Stage 3: after Research1(Structure), before EvolveStep. Skipped when protocol_mode='none'.
protocol_alignment_rules.backward_compatibility: Programs created before protocol_alignment_rules skip ProtocolAlign with no penalty. Existing pipelines without ProtocolAlign continue to operate identically.
protocol_alignment_rules.agent_card_independence: Agent Card generation was decoupled from ProtocolAlign in P11. ProtocolAlign skip (classification d: none-of-above) no longer affects agent_card.json output. Agent Card is generated unconditionally in the Generate stage.

agent_card_spec.description: Specification for Agent Card generation from AIAP program metadata. Agent Card is a standardized machine-readable self-description of an AIAP program for discovery, routing, and interoperability. Agent Card generation is UNCONDITIONAL — all programs regardless of Pattern produce agent_card.json.

extended_frontmatter_fields.skills: Array of skill objects derived from NLU intents — each skill has id, name, description, inputModes, outputModes
extended_frontmatter_fields.authentication: Security scheme reference from STANDARD.security security_schemes, mapped by trust_level
extended_frontmatter_fields.url: A2A endpoint URL — populated at deployment time, omitted during design

agent_card_derivation.source: AIAP.md frontmatter + main.aisop.json NLU intents + STANDARD.security security_schemes
rules: skills[] <- main NLU intent categories, each mapped to A2A Skill with inputModes/outputModes inferred from tools[]
capabilities <- capabilities_spec if declared, else { streaming: false, pushNotifications: false, stateTransitionHistory: false }
authentication <- security_schemes binding by trust_level (T1->none, T2->bearer, T3->oauth2, T4->oauth2+mutualTls)

generation_rules.trigger: UNCONDITIONAL — every Create and Evolve pipeline run
generation_rules.on_create: Generate from AIAP.md frontmatter + main.aisop.json NLU intents
generation_rules.on_evolve: Regenerate from updated AIAP.md to keep in sync
generation_rules.relationship_to_protocol_align: Agent Card generation is INDEPENDENT of ProtocolAlign. ProtocolAlign handles MCP/A2A protocol-level configurations (mcp_config.json, bridge_config.json). Agent Card handles program-level self-description.

output_schema.backward_compatibility: Programs without agent_card.json continue to function. agent_card.json is generated by the pipeline but not required for AISOP runtime execution.

agent_card_spec.backward_compatibility: Programs without agent_card_spec generate minimal Agent Card with name, description, and version only. Missing skills[] defaults to empty array. Missing capabilities defaults to all-false.

safety_card.description: Safety information section within agent_card.json. Addresses MIT CSAIL finding that 87% of AI agents lack safety documentation.
safety_card.risk_level_derivation: Derived from trust_level mapping: T1→minimal, T2→limited, T3→high, T4→high. Programs with crisis/medical/financial intents automatically elevated to 'high'.
safety_card.data_handling_derivation: Extracted from memory module (if exists): storage_type, retention_policy, encryption_level. If no memory module, default: {storage: 'none', retention: 'session', encryption: 'n/a'}.
safety_card.compliance_markers_derivation: Populated during Research3 (compliance mode). Contains references to applicable standards from spec_version_pinned.

task_lifecycle.description: Standardized execution state model for AIAP pipeline tasks. Aligned with A2A Task states for interoperability.

states.input_required: Task paused awaiting user input (e.g., LEVEL_A confirmation, QualityGate decision)
states.completed: Task finished successfully with all gates passed
states.failed: Task terminated due to unrecoverable error
states.cancelled: Task terminated by user cancellation request

transitions: working -> cancelled (user cancellation honored at stage boundary)
input_required -> cancelled (user cancels during input wait)

state_report_contract.state: string — current state from states enum
state_report_contract.progress: { completed_stages: number, total_stages: number, percentage: number }

task_lifecycle.mcp_tasks_alignment: AIAP task states map to MCP Tasks primitive: pending→working, active→working, input_required→input_required, completed→completed, failed→failed, cancelled→cancelled
task_lifecycle.backward_compatibility: Pipelines without task_lifecycle support continue to operate without state tracking. State reporting is opt-in via event_hooks configuration in pipeline_task.

operation_vocabulary.description: Standard AISOP Layer 2 operation verbs for semantic clarity in node function definitions. RECOMMENDED but not REQUIRED — existing free-text step descriptions remain valid.

operations.RUN: Execute a defined function or computation locally. Example: RUN ThreeDimTest on generated files.
operations.DELEGATE: Pass execution to another AISOP module (sub-module invocation). Example: DELEGATE to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='protocol'.
operations.ROUTE: Direct flow to one of multiple branches based on condition. Example: ROUTE by NLU intent classification result.
operations.GATE: Evaluate a pass/fail condition that controls pipeline flow. Example: GATE on quality_score >= quality_threshold.
operations.COLLECT: Gather outputs from multiple parallel or sequential operations. Example: COLLECT research_context from 3 Research modes.
operations.EMIT: Produce an output artifact or event for downstream consumption. Example: EMIT state_report via event_hooks.
operations.CLASSIFY: Categorize input into predefined categories with confidence scores. Example: CLASSIFY user intent into 13 NLU categories.

operation_vocabulary.usage_guidance: PREFER standard verbs at the beginning of step descriptions for clarity. Combine with domain-specific context: 'DELEGATE to advisor for security scheme generation'. Multiple verbs may appear in a single step when the step performs sequential operations.
operation_vocabulary.backward_compatibility: Operation vocabulary is purely advisory. Programs using free-text step descriptions without standard verbs receive no penalty in ThreeDimTest. Standard verbs provide semantic consistency for tooling and analysis but do not change execution behavior.

multimodal_message.description: Data contract for multimodal message parts in AIAP programs that handle non-text inputs/outputs. Aligned with A2A Part structure.

text.description: Plain text content — default for all text-based interactions

file.fields: { type: 'file', mimeType: string, data: string (base64) | uri: string }
file.description: Binary file content — images, audio, documents

data.description: Structured data — JSON objects for machine consumption

mode_inference_rules: image_generation tool -> output FilePart with mimeType 'image/png'
tts (text-to-speech) tool -> output FilePart with mimeType 'audio/wav'
code_execution tool -> output DataPart with execution results
web_fetch tool -> output TextPart with extracted content
file_system read -> output FilePart if binary, TextPart if text
Default: TextPart for all unmatched tool outputs
multimodal_message.a2a_alignment: Each part_type maps directly to A2A Part: TextPart -> A2A TextPart, FilePart -> A2A FilePart, DataPart -> A2A DataPart. No transformation needed.
multimodal_message.backward_compatibility: Programs without multimodal_message support treat all inputs and outputs as text (TextPart). Existing text-only programs are unaffected.

capabilities_spec.description: Declaration of execution capabilities for AIAP programs. Enables capability-aware routing and A2A interoperability.

streaming.description: Program supports Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time progress updates

pushNotifications.description: Program supports push notifications for async completion events

stateTransitionHistory.description: Program maintains full state transition history accessible via API

async.description: Program supports async task execution with polling-based status retrieval

capabilities_spec.backward_compatibility: Programs without capabilities_spec declaration default to all capabilities false. This preserves existing synchronous-only execution behavior. Capabilities are declared in AIAP.md frontmatter and reflected in Agent Card generation.

security_authentication_spec.description: Specification for security authentication scheme binding in AIAP programs. Maps trust_level to appropriate authentication mechanisms.

scheme_object.type: string — scheme type identifier (none, apiKey, http, oauth2, openIdConnect, mutualTls)
scheme_object.flows: object|null — OAuth2 flow configuration (authorizationCode, clientCredentials, etc.)
scheme_object.in: string|null — where credential is passed (header, query, cookie) — for apiKey/http types

T3_verified.description: OAuth2 with authorization code flow — verified publisher programs

T4_certified.description: OAuth2 plus mutual TLS — certified enterprise programs

security_authentication_spec.agent_card_binding: Security scheme is included in Agent Card authentication field during agent_card_derivation. A2A clients use this to determine required authentication for task submission.
security_authentication_spec.backward_compatibility: Programs without explicit security_authentication_spec use trust_level to infer authentication. T1 programs default to no authentication. Missing trust_level defaults to T1 (public).

identity_signature_spec.description: Specification for cryptographic identity signing of AIAP programs. Provides program integrity verification and publisher authentication.

algorithm_support.primary: ES256 (ECDSA with P-256 curve and SHA-256) — recommended for broad compatibility

key_management.distribution: Public key URL declared in AIAP.md frontmatter identity.public_key_url field
key_management.rotation: Key rotation supported — new key published at same URL, old signatures remain valid until expiry

signing_process: Step 1: Compute SHA-256 hash of canonical program content (all .aisop.json files concatenated in alphabetical order)
Step 2: Construct JWS payload { hash, program_id, version, timestamp, publisher }
Step 3: Sign payload with publisher's private key using declared algorithm
Step 4: Store signature in AIAP.md frontmatter identity.signature.value as JWS Compact Serialization string
verification_flow: Step 1: Extract signature from AIAP.md identity.signature.value
Step 2: Fetch public key from identity.public_key_url
Step 3: Verify JWS signature using public key and declared algorithm
Step 4: Recompute content hash and compare with signed hash
Step 5: Check timestamp freshness (signature age < max_signature_age, default 365 days)
Step 6: Result: VERIFIED (all checks pass) | SIGNATURE_INVALID | HASH_MISMATCH | EXPIRED | KEY_UNAVAILABLE
identity_signature_spec.backward_compatibility: Signing is OPTIONAL. Programs without identity signatures are valid but receive UNSIGNED trust status. Verification never blocks program execution — results are advisory for trust decisions.

extension_registry.description: Registry of STANDARD extension modules. ThreeDimTest loads extensions dynamically based on program profile. Supports both file-based (built-in) and URI-based (remote) extensions.

security.load_condition: always — security checks are mandatory for all programs

ecosystem.scope: MF10-MF14, MF16, K1-K10, PL16-PL17, PL22, PL25, simulation_categories F-M, packaging_specification, discovery_protocol, a2a_bridge, skill_round_trip, ui_rendering_rules, yellow_persistence_tracking, tool_dirs_packaging, registry_endpoint_extension, minimum_scenarios_matrix
ecosystem.load_condition: Pattern B+ programs only — single-file Pattern A programs skip ecosystem checks

performance.scope: PL13-PL15, PL18, PL23-PL24, QRG1-QRG5, E.figurative, E.injection, streaming_benchmarks, token_budget_allocation
performance.load_condition: Pipeline operations only — standalone Validate/Simulate skip performance extension

runtime_extensions.scope: 7 RESERVED_KEYS (on_error, retry_policy, context_filter, output_mapping, map, join, constraints), node_type_inference, RE1-RE3 validation rules
runtime_extensions.load_condition: always — RESERVED_KEYS validation is mandatory for all programs with function-layer extensions

uri_scheme.description: URI-driven extension resolution mechanism. Allows AIAP programs to reference extensions by URI in addition to built-in file-based extensions.
resolution_order: local — check local file system for extension file (built-in extensions always resolved first)
URI — resolve extension from declared URI in AIAP.md frontmatter extensions[] field
remote — fetch from remote registry if URI resolution fails and registry_uri is configured
uri_scheme.cache: Resolved remote extensions cached locally with TTL=24 hours. Cache key: SHA-256(URI).

remote_extensions.description: Schema for URI-referenced extensions declared in AIAP.md frontmatter.

schema.type: string — extension type: 'data' (adds structured info to core objects), 'configuration' (adds config requirements), 'method' (defines new operations)
schema.description: string — human-readable description of the extension's purpose
schema.required: boolean — whether the program requires this extension to function (true = FAIL if unavailable, false = graceful degradation)
schema.schema_url: string|null — URL to JSON Schema defining the extension's data structure

remote_extensions.validation: Remote extensions MUST provide a JSON Schema at schema_url. Extensions without schema_url are treated as advisory-only (no structural validation).
remote_extensions.trust: Remote extensions inherit REMOTE_UNVERIFIED trust status until verified via governance_hash. Required extensions from unverified sources emit WARNING.

examples.description: Three-tier memory system (Working/Episodic/Semantic) for Pattern E programs
Pre-response toxicity scoring and PII redaction for safety-sensitive domains

activation.description: Extension activation lifecycle — when and how extensions are loaded.
activation.design_time: Extensions declared in AIAP.md frontmatter extensions[] field are activated during program design. Generate step recommends applicable extensions based on program pattern and capabilities.
activation.runtime: Executor loads declared extensions at program startup. Required extensions that fail to load block execution. Optional extensions that fail to load emit WARNING and continue with degraded feature set.
activation.conflict_resolution: If two extensions define overlapping rules for the same check item, the more restrictive rule takes precedence. Built-in file-based extensions always override URI-based extensions for the same scope.

extension_registry.backward_compatibility: File-based extensions (security, ecosystem, performance) continue to operate identically to pre-P8 versions. URI-based extensions are purely additive — programs without extensions[] in AIAP.md frontmatter are unaffected. The loading_protocol remains unchanged for built-in extensions.
extension_registry.loading_protocol: ThreeDimTest and ReadTemplate MUST: (1) always load core, (2) always load security, (3) always load runtime_extensions, (4) load ecosystem if Pattern >= B, (5) load performance if in pipeline context or quality_threshold is 'strict', (6) load URI-based extensions declared in AIAP.md frontmatter extensions[] if present. Extension loading is additive — core rules always apply, extensions add additional checks.
extension_registry.version_requirement: All loaded extensions MUST have the same template_version as core. Version mismatch is a loading error. URI-based extensions declare their compatible template_version in the extension manifest.

composite_node_rules.description: Rules for nodes that internally execute logic of multiple other defined functions without graph-level routing.
composite_node_rules.rule: A COMPOSITE NODE is a Mermaid node whose function definition internally executes the logic of 2+ other functions defined in the same file, without routing through those functions via graph edges. Composite nodes MUST: (1) Include 'COMPOSITE NODE:' annotation at the beginning of step1. (2) List all internally-executed functions by name. (3) Document partial-success handling (what happens if one internal sub-execution fails). (4) Use a descriptive Mermaid label that hints at composite nature (e.g., 'Full Combined Analysis' instead of generic 'Full Analysis').
composite_node_rules.detection: ThreeDimTest D4 detects: if a function's steps reference executing the logic of other named functions in the same file (e.g., 'execute AnalyzePatterns, DetectAnomalies, ForecastSpending, CategoryTrends') but the Mermaid graph does not route through those function nodes, it is a composite node. If COMPOSITE annotation is missing, flag as D4 issue.
composite_node_rules.enforcement: Generate MUST annotate composite nodes during generation. ThreeDimTest D4 verifies annotation presence.

routing_parameter_rules.description: Rules for parameters with routing semantics — parameters that control which path a ModeGate/router node takes.
routing_parameter_rules.sentinel_default: Parameters with routing semantics (e.g., operation_type, report_type, analysis_type) MUST NOT use a valid route value as their default. Instead, use a sentinel value ('auto' or empty string) that is distinct from all valid route values. This allows the receiving module to distinguish 'caller explicitly chose route X' from 'caller used default'. When the sentinel default is received, the module falls back to NLU/inference from user_request.
routing_parameter_rules.enforcement: Generate MUST use sentinel defaults for newly created routing parameters. ThreeDimTest D4 checks: if a parameter appears in a ModeGate/routing function as the routing criterion, verify its default value is not identical to any valid route value listed in that function's step1.

reference_data_rules.description: Rules for managing large reference data sets embedded in AISOP step instructions.
reference_data_rules.threshold: When a step instruction contains >20 discrete data items of the same type (e.g., keyword lists, locale mappings, category enumerations), the data SHOULD be externalized to a versioned reference file rather than hardcoded inline.

externalization_pattern.file_location: Same directory as the AISOP module that consumes the data.
externalization_pattern.schema: { schema_version: string, data_type: string, items: array, language_coverage: [string]|null, last_updated: string(ISO 8601) }
externalization_pattern.loading: Step instruction references the file by name and specifies runtime loading: 'Load {filename} at runtime. If file missing, use inline fallback list (minimum safe subset).'
externalization_pattern.versioning: Reference file has independent version from AISOP module. Updates to reference data (e.g., adding keywords, new language) do NOT require AISOP version bump. Reference file version tracked in AIAP.md or module header.

reference_data_rules.deployer_override: Reference data files MAY include a deployer_override_path field. If set, the executor loads deployer-provided data from that path instead of the bundled file, enabling localization and customization without editing AISOP source.
reference_data_rules.enforcement: Generate SHOULD externalize when threshold exceeded. ThreeDimTest D7 (metabolism) checks: if a step contains >20 inline data items, flag as D7-INLINE-DATA advisory. Not a hard failure — inline data is acceptable for small/stable lists (<20 items).
reference_data_rules.exemptions: Enumeration types with fixed protocol-defined values (e.g., LEVEL_A/B/C, Pattern A-F, trust_level T1-T4) are exempt — these are structural constants, not reference data.

sub_routing_rules.description: Rules for sub-routes defined within NLU/router functions that map secondary intent types to existing nodes without adding Mermaid graph nodes.
sub_routing_rules.rule: A SUB-ROUTE is an intent-to-node mapping defined in a routing function's step text (typically NLU step2) that handles non-primary intent categories by reusing existing response nodes with modified behavior modes. Sub-routes do NOT create new Mermaid nodes — they are internal routing logic within the NLU function.
sub_routing_rules.requirements: (1) Each sub-route must specify: trigger patterns (signal words/phrases), target node name, and behavior mode (e.g., 'self-correction mode', 'task-attempt mode'). (2) Each sub-route must have a fallback for edge cases (e.g., MultiTurnContinuation with no prior context → route to Clarify). (3) Sub-routes must not conflict with primary routes — if a sub-route's trigger patterns overlap with a primary intent's signals, add a disambiguation rule or confusion pair.
sub_routing_rules.when_to_use: Use sub-routing when an intent category is (a) infrequent enough that a dedicated Mermaid node would be underutilized, (b) semantically close to an existing node's capability, and (c) can be handled by an existing node with a behavior modifier. Common sub-route types: FeedbackMeta (user correcting AI), TaskRequest (capability-bounded task attempts), MultiTurnContinuation (context continuation).
sub_routing_rules.enforcement: ThreeDimTest D5 checks sub-routes: verify each sub-route has trigger patterns, target node, behavior mode, and edge-case fallback. Generate should prefer sub-routing over new nodes when the candidate intent meets all three criteria in 'when_to_use'.

multi_file_guidelines.description: Quality rules for Pattern C/D multi-file AISOPs.

rules.MF1: All files in same directory must share consistent version numbers.
rules.MF2: Main file's delegation targets must match sub-module ids exactly.
rules.MF3: Shared parameters (e.g. workspace_dir, quality_threshold) must have same type and compatible defaults across all files.
rules.MF4: Cross-file data contracts: (a) FORMAT — output format of one module must match expected input format of the consuming module. (b) NAME — parameter names used in main router's delegation steps must exactly match the receiving sub-module's declared parameter names (e.g., if main passes operation='analyze_turn', sub-module must declare parameter named 'operation', not 'analysis_type'). (c) VALUE — parameter values passed by main must be valid values accepted by the receiving sub-module's routing logic (e.g., if main passes operation='analyze_turn', sub-module's routing function must list 'analyze_turn' as a valid route). (d) PATH — file path patterns referenced across modules must be consistent: if module A writes to {dir}/working.json, module B that reads the same data must use the identical path template {dir}/working.json (not {dir}/working_{id}.json or other variants). Check: for each file_system write in any module, trace all corresponding reads across all modules and verify path templates match exactly. Generate and Modify MUST verify MF4 (a/b/c/d) during multi-file operations.
rules.MF5: Module ID to filename: the segment after the last '.' in module id maps to filename. E.g., id='travel_planner.search' -> search.aisop.json. id='travel_planner.main' -> main.aisop.json. DependencyCheck uses this rule to resolve module IDs to file paths.
rules.MF6: AIAP.md version field must match main.aisop.json version field. AIAP.md modules list must match actual .aisop.json files in directory. NODE COUNT VERIFICATION: for each module listed in AIAP.md modules[], if a 'nodes' field is present, verify it matches the actual functional node count in the corresponding .aisop.json file (functional nodes = total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode). Mismatch is MF6-NODE-COUNT. Generate and ReviewFinalize MUST auto-compute and update AIAP.md node counts during AIAP.md write.
rules.MF7: Routing parameter symmetry: in Pattern B+ architectures, if existing sub-modules receive explicit routing parameters from the main router (e.g., operation_type for record, report_type for report), newly added sub-modules with internal routing decisions (ModeGate/TypeGate nodes) SHOULD receive an equivalent explicit routing parameter. This ensures deterministic routing and prevents duplicate NLU parsing in sub-modules. Check: for each sub-module with a ModeGate/routing decision node, verify it has a dedicated routing parameter passed from main, OR document the design choice to use user_request-based inference.
rules.MF8: Semantic operation consistency: within a module, operations of the same semantic type SHOULD route through the same node type. Specifically: all delete operations (expense delete, template delete, etc.) should converge to the module's delete node; all save/create/update operations should converge to the save node. When adding new paths, check existing paths with similar operation semantics and route through the same node types for architectural consistency. Check: identify operation semantics (create/read/update/delete) for each ModeGate route, verify same-type operations converge to same-type target nodes.
rules.MF9: Cross-module node invocation (bidirectional): for Pattern B+ architectures, verify BOTH directions of module connectivity. FORWARD CHECK: every parameter value main router passes to a sub-module must trigger a valid route in that sub-module's routing gate — if main passes operation='X' but sub-module has no route for 'X', it is MF9-FORWARD violation. REVERSE CHECK: every routing branch declared in a sub-module's routing gate (ModeGate, OperationGate, PhaseGate) must be reachable via at least one caller's delegation — if sub-module declares route 'Y' but no caller ever passes the parameter value that triggers 'Y', the route is a CROSS-MODULE ORPHAN (MF9-REVERSE violation). Check: for each sub-module, (1) identify all entry points from callers' delegation and trace parameter values passed, (2) for each sub-module routing node verify every branch has at least one triggering caller, (3) for each caller delegation verify the target route exists in sub-module. EXEMPTION: operations not triggered by standard main router delegation are exempt when annotated with their invocation context. Annotation taxonomy: '(user-initiated)' for operations invoked by direct user request (e.g., data deletion, export, manual crisis_check); '(event-triggered)' for operations invoked by runtime conditions rather than router dispatch (e.g., session-end summary, proactive recall triggered by session state); '(scheduled)' for operations invoked by time-based triggers (e.g., periodic decay, consolidation cron). Each annotation suppresses MF9-REVERSE warning for that route. Unannotated orphan routes remain violations.
rules.MF15: Output Contract Enforcement: every module output passed to the next pipeline stage MUST conform to its declared json_schema in STANDARD.data_contracts. Check: at each pipeline stage boundary (Research→Evolve, Generate→QualityGate, etc.), validate output against json_schema. Validation: (a) required fields present, (b) field types match, (c) enum constraints satisfied. Mode: WARNING by default; FAIL via quality_threshold='strict'. Contracts json_schema rules: MUST TRY to declare accurate json_schema. Failure to declare does not block or penalize.
rules.MF16: Tool Directory Consistency (Pattern G): verify (a) tool_dirs[].mcp_server references an existing mcp_server.json file, (b) mcp_server.json exposed_tools[].name entries have corresponding AIAP.md tools[] entries, (c) AIAP.md tools[] entries with mcp_server.server_id='file:///./mcp_tools' have corresponding mcp_server.json exposed_tools declarations, (d) if a2a_tools/agent_card.json exists, its aiap_extension.governance_hash must match AIAP.md governance_hash. N/A for programs without tool_dirs. SUPERSEDED: This base definition (a-d) is extended by AIAP_Standard.ecosystem MF16 (a-h) which adds aiap_tools directory checks. When ecosystem extension is loaded, use ecosystem MF16 as canonical.

MF17.name: Mandatory Output Completeness
MF17.check: All UNCONDITIONAL and MANDATORY outputs declared in STANDARD exist in target directory after pipeline completion. Mandatory outputs: (a) AIAP.md — governance contract, (b) quality_baseline.json — quality scores and regression baseline, (c) agent_card.json — A2A discovery card with safety_card (UNCONDITIONAL for ALL patterns per P11, not Pattern G only), (d) .version_history/{version}/meta.json — version metadata snapshot. For Evolve/Modify pipelines additionally: (e) .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json — pipeline learning record. RECOMMENDED outputs (not BLOCK, WARNING only): (f) .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json — pipeline learning record for learning loop. Missing creation_experience emits WARNING but does not block governance_hash.
MF17.severity: BLOCK — pipeline MUST NOT compute governance_hash with missing mandatory outputs. ReviewFinalize checks MF17 before PL14 hash computation.
MF17.auto_remediation: agent_card.json can be auto-generated from AIAP.md metadata (name, version, capabilities.offered, tools). Other missing files require pipeline re-execution.
MF17.distinction_from_MF16: MF16 validates Pattern G tool directory structure. MF17 validates universal mandatory outputs for ALL patterns.

MF18.name: Mechanism Conflict Scan
MF18.check: Within each function, identify steps that modify the same output target (e.g., response_text, final_output, appended prompts). If 3+ steps in the same function all append/modify the same output, verify mutual exclusion controls exist: (a) Explicit exclusion clauses ('if step X already fired, SKIP', 'max 1 per turn', priority ordering). (b) Frequency controls (cooldown, session quota, turn limit). (c) Conditional gating (mood check, context check, probability gate). If no mutual exclusion found between co-located modification steps: flag YELLOW 'MF18-MECHANISM-CONFLICT: steps [{step_ids}] in {function} all modify {output_target} without mutual exclusion — recommend adding exclusion matrix.' Generate conflict matrix: step × step → {compatible, exclusive, conditional}. SCOPE: applies per-function, not per-module. Only flags when 3+ steps target the same output — 2-step co-modification is common and manageable. EVOLVE MODE: when evolution adds new steps that modify an existing output target, check against ALL existing steps targeting that output, not just new ones.
MF18.severity: YELLOW (advisory) — mechanism conflicts are design decisions, not structural errors. Flag for human review.
MF18.evidence: soulbot_chat v1.8.0: 3 growth mechanisms (Socratic step3_6, metacognitive step3_7, growth nudge step3_8) all modified response_text without mutual exclusion. Caught in simulation (C5-R1), should have been caught at Generate time.

MF20.name: Cross-Module Signal Consumption Audit
MF20.check: For Pattern B+ programs with 2+ modules: scan all function steps for signal emission patterns ('emit', 'signal', 'pass to {module}', 'send to', 'output.*for {module}', 'include.*in.*delegation'). For each detected emission, extract signal_name and target_module. Then scan target_module's functions for consumption patterns ('consume', 'receive', 'load', 'check', 'if.*{signal_name}', 'read.*{signal_name}'). RESULT: if signal emitted but no consumer found in target module → RED 'MF20-SIGNAL-ORPHAN: {source_module}.{function}.{step} emits {signal_name} targeting {target_module} but no consumption step found'. If signal_name spelling differs between emitter and consumer (fuzzy match > 0.8 but not exact) → YELLOW 'MF20-SIGNAL-MISMATCH: emitter uses {name_a}, consumer uses {name_b}'. SKIP for single-module programs or Create mode.
MF20.severity: RED (signal orphan — data flow broken) / YELLOW (name mismatch — potential bug)
MF20.evidence: soulbot_chat v1.9.0: ScenarioTrainer (conversation) step4 emits scenario_completed with dimension_deltas targeting profiler. profiler.ExtractSignals had no step consuming this signal — growth dimension updates from scenario training were silently lost. Required manual ModifyStep to add step3_9_5.

MF21.name: Intra-Function Step Mutual Exclusion Audit
MF21.check: Within each function, identify steps that modify the same output target (response_text, final_output, user_message, etc.) using semantic patterns ('modify response', 'append to response', 'adjust response', 'inject into response', 'prepend to response', 'replace response').
