If 3+ steps target the same output: (a) check for shared state tracking variable (gate/applied/fired/count patterns: 'intervention_applied', 'steps_applied', 'gate_check', '{feature}_fired'), (b) check for explicit mutual exclusion clause ('if {gate} already set, SKIP', 'max 1 per turn', 'mutual exclusion'). No shared state AND no exclusion clause → YELLOW 'MF21-MUTEX-MISSING: steps [{step_ids}] in {function} all modify {output_target} without shared gate variable. Consider adding a mutual exclusion gate step.' RELATIONSHIP to MF18: MF18 detects cross-step output conflicts and generates conflict matrix. MF21 is the enforcement check — verifies that conflicts detected by MF18 have mitigation mechanisms. MF21 runs AFTER MF18 and consumes MF18's conflict_matrix. If MF18 found no conflicts, MF21 is automatically PASS.
MF21.severity: YELLOW (advisory — mutual exclusion is a design decision, but absence with 3+ competing steps is likely a bug)
MF21.evidence: soulbot_chat v1.9.0: conversation.AdaptResponse had step3_6 (Socratic), step3_7 (metacognition), step3_8 (nudge), step3_9 (resilience) — all 4 steps could modify response_text with growth interventions. No shared tracking variable existed. Required manual addition of step3_5_5 Growth Intervention Gate with growth_intervention_applied flag.

MF22.name: Safety Gate Signal Enforcement Audit
MF22.check: For programs with a dedicated safety module (safety.aisop.json or module with 'safety' in id): scan safety module's output steps for gate/throttle/block signals — patterns: 'throttle', 'block', 'suppress', 'gate', 'limit', 'restrict', 'engaged', 'flag.*true'. For each detected safety gate signal, identify the target module (from signal context or data flow). Scan target module's functions for explicit response to that signal — patterns: 'if {signal_name}', 'check {signal_name}', 'when {signal_name}', 'respond to {signal_name}'. RESULT: if safety gate signal has no downstream response → RED 'MF22-SAFETY-IGNORED: safety.{function}.{step} emits {signal_name} but {target_module} has no step checking this signal — safety directive unenforced'. ESCALATION: MF22 RED items are non-waivable in PostSimulateGate — safety signal enforcement failures cannot be accepted as YELLOW. SKIP for programs without safety module.
MF22.severity: RED (non-waivable — safety signals being ignored is a compliance risk per STANDARD.security)
MF22.evidence: soulbot_chat v1.9.0: safety.ScreenOutput.step5_9 computed growth_throttle_engaged=true and output to metadata. But conversation.AdaptResponse had no step checking this signal — safety module correctly detected user fatigue but conversation module completely ignored the suppression directive. Required manual ModifyStep to add throttle response in AdaptResponse.step3_5_5.

MF23.name: Name-Version Embedded Consistency
MF23.check: For each .aisop.json file: extract the 'name' field from aisop metadata. If name contains a version pattern (regex: /v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+/), extract the embedded version string. Compare with the 'version' field value. RESULT: if embedded version != version field → RED 'MF23-NAME-VERSION-MISMATCH: {file} name field contains version {embedded} but version field is {actual}'. If name does not contain a version pattern → PASS (no version embedding requirement). SCOPE: all .aisop.json files in target program. RUN TIMING: ValidateStep (alongside MF1).
MF23.severity: RED (version inconsistency indicates stale update — name field was not synced during last evolution)
MF23.evidence: soulbot_chat v1.9.0: all 5 .aisop.json files had name='soulbot_chat v1.8.0' but version='1.9.0'. The v1.8.0→v1.9.0 evolution updated version fields but forgot to sync the version embedded in name fields. Discovered during v1.10.0 evolution ModifyStep.

MF24.name: Protocol Document Consistency
MF24.check: For programs with domain protocol dependencies (AIAP.md dependencies[] with required=true): when LEVEL_A/B changes introduce new message types, new tools, new behaviors, or new protocol sections, scan the domain protocol document for corresponding coverage. METHOD: (1) Extract new_entities from evolution_plan: new enum values (message types, event types), new tool declarations, new intent categories. (2) For each new_entity, search domain protocol document for a section or table entry covering that entity. (3) RESULT: if new_entity has no corresponding protocol coverage → YELLOW 'MF24-PROTOCOL-GAP: evolution adds {entity_type} "{entity_name}" but domain protocol {protocol_file} has no corresponding section. Protocol document may need update.' (4) If 3+ entities lack coverage → escalate to RED 'MF24-PROTOCOL-STALE: {N} new entities introduced without protocol documentation. Domain protocol requires update.' SKIP for programs without domain protocol dependencies or for Create mode (protocol created alongside program).
MF24.severity: YELLOW (single gap) / RED (3+ gaps — protocol document is stale)
MF24.evidence: ai_social v1.2.0: evolution added 5 Web Presence message types and web_browser tool, requiring AISP_Protocol.md update with §28-30. Creator had no check to detect this gap — protocol update was manual.

MF25.name: Tool Integration Completeness
MF25.check: When tools[] is modified (additions or removals) during evolution: verify all touchpoints are updated. For each ADDED tool: (a) MF25a — at least one module's tools[] includes the new tool AND at least one function step references it. If no module uses it → YELLOW 'MF25-PHANTOM-TOOL: tool "{name}" added to AIAP.md but no module declares or uses it.' (b) MF25b — AIAP.md tools[] table includes the tool with annotations. If missing → RED 'MF25-AIAP-MISSING: tool "{name}" used by module {module} but not declared in AIAP.md tools[].' (c) MF25c — if trust_level >= 3, verify trust/safety modules have governance rules covering the tool (constraints mentioning tool name or tool category). If no governance → YELLOW 'MF25-UNGOVERNED: tool "{name}" has no governance rules in trust or safety modules.' (d) MF25d — tool annotations (read_only, destructive, idempotent, open_world) are consistent across all modules declaring the tool. If inconsistent → RED 'MF25-ANNOTATION-CONFLICT: tool "{name}" has different annotations in {module_a} vs {module_b}.' For REMOVED tools: verify no module still references the removed tool. SKIP for single-module programs or when tools[] unchanged.
MF25.severity: YELLOW (phantom/ungoverned) / RED (missing declaration or annotation conflict)
MF25.evidence: ai_social v1.2.0: adding web_browser required coordinated updates across main (declaration), web_social (usage), AIAP.md (tool table), trust (permissions), safety (governance). No automated check verified all touchpoints.

MF26.name: Enum Registry Consistency
MF26.check: For Pattern B+ programs with shared enum-like parameter values across modules: build consolidated enum registry by scanning all module parameters for 'enum' fields. For each enum that appears in 2+ modules (matched by semantic role — e.g., message_type, event_type, action_type): (1) BUILD REGISTRY — collect all unique values from each module's enum definition. (2) COVERAGE CHECK — for each value in the registry, verify it appears in all modules that should reference it. Determine 'should reference' by module role: if module defines the enum (e.g., messaging defines message_type) = AUTHORITATIVE; if module consumes the enum (e.g., trust checks permissions per message_type) = CONSUMER. (3) RESULT: value in AUTHORITATIVE but missing from CONSUMER → YELLOW 'MF26-ENUM-GAP: {authoritative_module} defines {enum_name}="{value}" but {consumer_module} has no corresponding rule/entry.' Value in CONSUMER but not in AUTHORITATIVE → YELLOW 'MF26-ENUM-ORPHAN: {consumer_module} references {enum_name}="{value}" but {authoritative_module} does not define it — possible stale reference.' SKIP for single-module programs or programs without shared enums.
MF26.severity: YELLOW (gap or orphan — enum values may be inconsistently handled across modules)
MF26.evidence: ai_social v1.2.0: 5 new message types added to messaging but needed corresponding entries in trust (permission levels) and safety (audit rules). No automated check verified cross-module enum coverage.

MF27.name: New Module Dependency Symmetry
MF27.check: For LEVEL_A changes that add new modules (EVOLVE mode only): compare the new module's cross-module dependency set against existing modules' dependency sets. METHOD: (1) For each existing module, extract dependency_set = set of other modules it calls (patterns: 'Call {program}.{module}', 'delegate to {module}', 'pass to {module}'). (2) For the new module, extract new_dependency_set similarly. (3) Compute common_dependencies = intersection of dependency_sets across existing modules. (4) MISSING COMMON CHECK: if new module OMITS a dependency that ≥50% of existing modules call → YELLOW 'MF27-MISSING-COMMON: new module "{new_module}" does not call "{common_dep}" (called by {N}/{total} existing modules). Verify this omission is intentional.' (5) UNIQUE DEPENDENCY CHECK: if new module calls a module that NO existing module calls → YELLOW 'MF27-UNIQUE-DEP: new module "{new_module}" uniquely depends on "{unique_dep}" (no other module calls it). Verify this dependency is correct.' Both checks are advisory (YELLOW) — asymmetric dependencies are sometimes intentional. SKIP for Create mode or when no new modules added.
MF27.severity: YELLOW (advisory — asymmetric dependencies may be intentional but warrant verification)
MF27.evidence: ai_social v1.2.0: new web_social module called safety, trust, and reputation modules. MF27 would have verified this matches the dependency pattern of existing modules.

MF28.name: Node Gate Completeness
MF28.check: For programs meeting applicability: (a) every non-start node's S1 contains [ASSERT]. (b) [ASSERT] references correct predecessor node(s) derived from Mermaid graph TD edges — every edge A --> B or A -- label --> B makes A a predecessor of B. (c) multi-predecessor nodes (2+ incoming Mermaid edges) list ALL incoming edge sources joined by ∨ in the predicate. (d) primary predecessor (backtrack target after 'go back to') is the main forward path node — typically the first edge in topological order, excluding loop-back and error-recovery edges. (e) backtrack target after 'go back to' is a concrete non-empty node name (not '.' or empty). (f) terminal node uses endNode((End)) format.
MF28.applicability: node_count >= 6 OR program contains QualityGate node OR self-evolution pipeline OR trust_level >= T3. For 3-5 nodes: RECOMMENDED. For 1-2 nodes: OPTIONAL.
MF28.relationship: Enforces Appendix F (Node Gate) mechanism. Complements strict_semantics (global intent) with per-node execution assertions. See AIAP_Protocol §F for full mechanism description including backtrack rules and AgentSpec three-tuple mapping.

MF28b.name: Node Gate Required Consistency
MF28b.check: If [ASSERT] includes Required clause: verify (a) declared Required fields exist in predecessor node's execution record (cache), (b) step text data references align with Required declaration — fields used in step but not in Required → YELLOW advisory (implicit dependency), fields in Required but not used in step → YELLOW advisory (redundant declaration).
MF28b.applicability: Only when [ASSERT] contains Required clause. Programs without Required are not checked. OPTIONAL — does not block execution.
MF28b.relationship: Extends MF28 (Node Gate Completeness). MF28 checks ASSERT existence and predecessor correctness; MF28b checks ASSERT Required data accuracy. Independent — MF28b failure does not imply MF28 failure. See AIAP_Protocol §F.2 for Required syntax and §F.9 for compliance summary.

MF29.name: Version Sync
MF29.check: AIAP.md.version == all .aisop.json version == agent_card.json.version == quality_baseline.json.version. Check: extract version from each file, compare all values. Any mismatch is violation.
MF29.auto_remediation: Auto-correct all files to AIAP.md version value. AIAP.md is authoritative source for version.
MF29.evidence: soulbot_chat v1.9.0: all 5 .aisop.json files had name='soulbot_chat v1.8.0' but version='1.9.0'. Discovered during v1.10.0 evolution.

MF30.name: Score Consistency
MF30.check: AIAP.md.quality.weighted_score == quality_baseline.three_dim_test.weighted_score. quality_baseline.json is authoritative source. Check: load both files, compare weighted_score values.
MF30.auto_remediation: AIAP.md.quality.weighted_score auto-corrects to quality_baseline value. quality_baseline is never modified by MF30.

MF31.name: Mermaid-Function Consistency
MF31.check: For each .aisop.json with Mermaid graph: (a) every rectangle node in Mermaid graph TD MUST have a matching key in functions{}. (b) every functions{} key MUST appear as a node in Mermaid graph. (c) diamond nodes ({...?}) are exempt — they represent decisions within parent nodes, not independent functions. (d) PascalCase diamond nodes suggesting independent nodes are AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: rename to lowercase or merge into parent. (e) terminal node MUST use endNode((End)) format — variants (End, end, Finish) are non-standard and AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: normalize to endNode((End)).
MF31.auto_remediation: PascalCase diamonds and non-standard terminal node names are AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE with HIGH confidence. Auto-fix: (d) rename to camelCase, (e) rename to endNode((End)).

MF32.name: sys.io.confirm Immutability
MF32.check: For evolving programs: sys.io.confirm steps in version N must all exist in version N+1. Only additions allowed, not deletions or modifications.
MF32.how_to_verify: Scan all .aisop.json files in version N (source): extract every step text starting with 'sys.io.confirm'. Scan version N+1 (target) likewise. Comparison key = first argument (message string), scanned across all files (not bound to node name or step position). Semantically equivalent rewording reviewed by user in ReviewPresent. Complete disappearance of a message = deletion = violation. Do NOT rely on AIAP.md counts — always scan source files.
MF32.exception: If the entire node containing sys.io.confirm is removed (function removal), the removal must be confirmed via sys.io.confirm or explicit user approval in the evolution pipeline.
MF32.reference: AIAP Protocol Appendix G.2, AISOP V1.0.0 §6.2

MF33.name: Step Mode Exclusivity
MF33.check: Each step text must be exactly one of: (1) sys.* system call, (2) RUN aisop.* sub-task invocation, (3) natural language instruction. Mixing modes in a single step is not allowed.
MF33.how_to_verify: For each step in functions: classify step text by prefix — sys.* | RUN aisop.* | other. If step contains sys.* AND natural language (excluding [ASSERT] prefix), flag as mixed mode. [ASSERT] prefix is exempt — it is an AIAP governance marker, not a sys.* call.
MF33.reference: AISOP V1.0.0 §5.3 one-step-one-mode rule, AIAP Protocol Appendix G.9

MF34.name: sys.assert Expression Validity
MF34.check: sys.assert expressions must conform to AISOP §6.11 deterministic expression engine syntax.
MF34.how_to_verify: For each sys.assert step: extract expression string (first argument). Verify expression uses only allowed operators: ==, !=, >, <, >=, <=, &&, ||, !, != null, == null, . (member access). Flag expressions containing function calls, arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /), or string operations as invalid.
MF34.reference: AISOP V1.0.0 §6.11, AIAP Protocol Appendix G.3

MF35.name: Return Value Consistency
MF35.check: sys.* return values (-> variable) must have unique names within a node and must only be referenced in subsequent steps of the same node.
MF35.how_to_verify: For each function: (a) collect all -> variable_name declarations. (b) verify no duplicate names within the same function. (c) for sys.* steps referencing variables, verify the variable was declared in a prior step of the same function. (d) flag references to variables from other functions (cross-node reference without output_mapping or sys.state). Note: variable references in natural language steps are best-effort detection only — static analysis reliably covers sys.* formal references but not natural language mentions.
MF35.reference: AISOP V1.0.0 §6.9, AIAP Protocol Appendix G.9

MF36.name: sys.* Permission Consistency
MF36.check: sys.run, sys.code.exec, sys.io.read/write, sys.llm calls must be consistent with AIAP.md permissions declarations.
MF36.how_to_verify: For each sys.* call in functions: (a) sys.run → verify permissions.shell.allowed is true. (b) sys.code.exec → verify code_execution.allowed is true (or shell.allowed if code_execution not declared). (c) sys.io.read/write with literal file paths → verify paths are within permissions.file_system.scope; variable paths (-> references) flagged as UNCHECKED (runtime verification required). (d) sys.llm with external model → verify permissions.network.allowed is true. Mismatch = security violation.

pipeline_rules.description: Full Pipeline execution rules for Create/Evolve operations.

rules.PL1: Create/Evolve MUST execute full pipeline: Research(structure)→Evolve→Generate→Research(quality)→Modify→Generate→QualityGate→Research(compliance)→Validate→Simulate→Review, and generate/update AIAP.md.
rules.PL2: Research is shared module called 3 times with different modes: structure (before Evolve), quality (before Modify), compliance (before Validate).
rules.PL3: QualityGate failure loops back to ModifyStep, max 2 retries then force pass with WARNING.
rules.PL5: Validate/Simulate/Explain/General are standalone operations — do NOT trigger pipeline.
rules.PL6: Pipeline context threading: old_version_context and pipeline_metadata are initialized by PipelineStart and accumulated across all pipeline steps. Each step appends its metrics to pipeline_metadata. ReviewPresent receives both for summary generation.
rules.PL7: Output naming: Research module ALWAYS outputs as research_context (never compliance_context or structure_report). Use research_mode field to distinguish content.
rules.PL8: Incremental Pipeline: for Pattern B+ evolution, hash each sub-module's content before and after Generate. If a sub-module's hash is unchanged from old_version, downstream stages (Modify, Simulate detail traces) MAY skip that sub-module. Stage-skip decisions recorded in pipeline_metadata.stage_hashes.
rules.PL9: Quality Baseline: ReviewFinalize MUST persist quality_baseline to .version_history/{version}/quality_baseline.json on successful pipeline completion. PipelineStart MUST load previous baseline (if exists) and pass to SimulateStep for regression detection.
rules.PL10: Stage Output Validation (per stage): each Pipeline stage MUST validate its received inputs from prior stages before processing. Validation: (a) null check — if expected input is null/empty when it should contain data, log warning and trigger stage-specific fallback. (b) schema check — verify input matches STANDARD.data_contracts schema (e.g., research_context must have domain + verified_findings + verified_count). (c) retry policy — if validation fails, retry input retrieval once; if still failing, execute stage fallback (documented per stage). Fallbacks: Research output null → AI-knowledge with 'unverified' label; Modify input null → internal search; Validate/Simulate input null → inline execution with warning.
rules.PL11: AIAP.md Lifecycle: PipelineStart MUST verify AIAP.md exists in target directory. If missing (new creation), Generate1 creates AIAP.md with all required fields. If exists (evolution/modification), ReviewFinalize updates version, modules, quality fields after finalization. AIAP.md version MUST match final .aisop.json version.
rules.PL12: Pipeline Stage Enforcement Levels: MANDATORY — stage MUST execute, skip only on technical failure (file not found, invalid JSON), not on discretion. RECOMMENDED — stage SHOULD execute, skip requires explicit pipeline_metadata.skip_reason with justification. OPTIONAL — stage MAY be skipped freely. Stage classification: MANDATORY: PipelineStart, DependencyCheck, Generate1, ThreeDimTest, QualityGate, ReviewPresent/ReviewFinalize. RECOMMENDED: Research1, Research2, ModifyStep, Generate2, ValidateStep, SimulateStep. OPTIONAL: Research3, ObservabilityStep. Evolve mode override: SimulateStep escalates to MANDATORY for Evolve operations (evolution introduces higher regression risk). Skip tracking: any RECOMMENDED stage skipped → record in pipeline_metadata.skipped_stages[] with reason. ReviewPresent MUST display skipped stages prominently with warning banner.
rules.PL19: Elicitation Protocol: Pipeline gates that require human input SHOULD emit standardized elicitation requests per STANDARD.data_contracts.elicitation. Applicable gates: (1) EvolveStep — LEVEL_A/B change presentation (type=form, fields per change item with accept/reject), (2) PostSimulateGate — RED issue handling (type=choice, options: auto-fix/proceed/abort), (3) AutoFixGate — auto-fix approval (type=confirm, default_action=approve), (4) QualityGate retry — retry/force-pass decision (type=choice). Elicitation is RECOMMENDED (not MANDATORY) — executors without structured UI support fall back to natural language presentation. When elicitation is used, include timeout_seconds and default_action for non-interactive pipeline runs. CANONICAL SOURCE: This is the authoritative definition. Performance extension references this rule by ID.
rules.PL20: Pipeline Progress Protocol: Pipeline orchestrator SHOULD maintain a pipeline_task object per STANDARD.data_contracts.pipeline_task throughout pipeline execution. (a) INITIALIZATION: PipelineStart creates pipeline_task with task_id=pipeline_run_id, status='running', total_stages from pipeline configuration. (b) STAGE TRANSITION: at each stage boundary, update current_stage, append completed stage to progress.completed_stages, recalculate percentage. (c) ESTIMATION: if pipeline_metadata.stage_timings contains historical data, compute estimated_remaining_seconds from average durations of remaining stages. (d) EMISSION: progress updates are advisory — pipeline execution continues regardless of progress reporting success. (e) RECORDING: final pipeline_task state recorded in pipeline_metadata for observability. CANONICAL SOURCE: This is the authoritative definition. Performance extension references this rule by ID.
rules.PL21: Pipeline Cancellation: Pipeline orchestrator SHOULD support cooperative cancellation via pipeline_task.cancel_requested flag. (a) CHECK FREQUENCY: check cancel_requested at each stage boundary (between stages, not mid-stage). Long-running stages (Research with multiple searches, Simulate with many scenarios) SHOULD also check at sub-step boundaries. (b) ON CANCEL: complete current stage if already in progress (do not interrupt mid-stage), save partial results to .version_history/ with status='cancelled' annotation, set pipeline_task.status='cancelled', report to user with list of completed stages and partial results location. (c) PARTIAL RESULTS: cancelled pipelines preserve all completed stage outputs — partial results are valid and can be resumed from. (d) COOPERATIVE: cancellation is cooperative, not preemptive — stages are never forcibly terminated. A stage may take up to its normal duration to honor cancellation. CANONICAL SOURCE: This is the authoritative definition. Performance extension references this rule by ID.

PL27.name: Pipeline Stage Severity Classification
PL27.rule: Pipeline stages classified by failure severity: CRITICAL (Generate, QualityGate, ReviewPresent, ReviewFinalize — failure aborts pipeline, no degraded continuation, max 3 retries then abort), IMPORTANT (Research1, EvolveStep, ModifyStep, SimulateStep, ValidateStep — failure triggers WARNING + degraded continuation allowed with user confirmation, max 2 retries then offer degraded path), OPTIONAL (Research2, Research3, ObservabilityStep, PostSimulateGate — failure skipped with INFO, pipeline continues, max 1 retry then skip). Each stage Error field references PL27 severity.
PL27.enforcement: main.aisop.json stage Error fields reference PL27 severity level

PL28.name: Self-Observation
PL28.companion: The verification logic for this rule is specified in AIAP_Standard.performance PL28 (same rule code, role=verification_rule). Both entries describe the same PL28 rule from complementary perspectives: this entry defines what a program must do; the performance entry defines how ThreeDimTest verifies compliance.
PL28.rule: When a program's insights mode is ON (insights.aisop.json exists as a module): UNIVERSAL COVERAGE — All intents (not just pipeline intents) MUST pass through a PostExecution convergence node before endNode. PostExecution triggers intent-level self-observation via insights.aisop.json (Layer 1). PIPELINE ENHANCEMENT (optional) — Pipeline intents (Create/Evolve) may additionally have per-stage parallel self-observation in the pipeline entry function (Layer 2 fine-grained). CODE-AS-SWITCH — PostExecution node existence = insights ON. PostExecution node absence = insights OFF. No separate switch field. ENABLEMENT — Creator advisor EnableInsights inserts PostExecution node and reroutes all intent terminal nodes. DisableInsights removes PostExecution and restores direct routing. Self-observation receives the execution context (inputs, outputs, errors, timing) and analyzes whether the execution exposed any deficiency in the program being used. Findings are buffered in memory (insight_buffer) and flushed to insights.json via PostExecution FlushToFile. Self-observation is non-blocking — it does NOT block progression to endNode. Coverage verification: ThreeDimTest checks PostExecution node existence and intent routing completeness (see AIAP_Standard.performance PL28 verification).
PL28.enforcement: main.aisop.json PostExecution convergence node presence = insights ON. PostExecution node absence = insights OFF. All intent terminal nodes must route to PostExecution when ON, and directly to endNode when OFF.
PL28.lifecycle: Insights ON/OFF is a LEVEL_A operation managed by Creator advisor insights sub-graph. ON = create insights.aisop.json + insert PostExecution convergence node (reroute all intents) + add PipelineEntry Layer 2 (if applicable) + add AIAP.md declaration. OFF = delete insights.aisop.json + remove PostExecution node (restore direct routing) + remove PipelineEntry Layer 2 + remove AIAP.md declaration + archive insights.json.

NO_CROSS_WRITE.name: Insight Attribution Rule
NO_CROSS_WRITE.rule: When program B is used to evolve/create target program C: (a) B writes to B's own insights.json (B is the program being used, B observes its own deficiencies). (b) C does NOT write insights (C is being evolved, not used). (c) C's quality issues are recorded in C's quality_baseline.remaining_yellow. Simplified: whoever's pipeline/runtime is running writes their own insights. Examples: Creator evolves ai_social → Creator writes Creator's insights.json. Agent runs ai_social → ai_social writes ai_social's insights.json.
NO_CROSS_WRITE.enforcement: ThreeDimTest verifies insights.json entries match the program's own id, not a target program's id.

research_credibility.description: 5-level credibility classification for research sources. Used by Research module to tag findings and by Auto-LEVEL classifier to weight confidence.

5.label: Legislative/Regulatory
5.description: Official government legislation, regulation text, executive orders (.gov, official gazette)

4.label: Academic/Peer-Reviewed
4.description: Peer-reviewed journals, RCTs, systematic reviews (arXiv with citations, Nature, NEJM, ACM, IEEE)

3.label: Industry Authority
3.description: Major tech companies, established research labs, standards bodies (NIST, ISO, Google Research, OpenAI)

2.label: Professional Analysis
2.description: Law firm analysis, consulting reports, established tech publications (National Law Review)

1.label: General/Blog
1.description: Personal blogs, social media, unverified sources (Medium, personal blogs)

research_credibility.usage: Research module assigns credibility_tier to each source in research_finding.source.credibility_tier. Auto-LEVEL classifier (B2) uses credibility_tier as confidence multiplier. Low-tier sources (1-2) require corroboration from tier >= 3 for LEVEL_B changes.
research_credibility.corroboration_rule: LEVEL_B changes sourced exclusively from tier <= 2 require at least one corroborating source from tier >= 3. Otherwise downgrade to LEVEL_C or flag for human judgment.

Dimension score = average of applicable item scores.

schema.effective_ttl_seconds: number — actual TTL used for this cache entry (may differ from base due to dynamic adjustment)

cache_policy.description: Dynamic cache TTL policy for pipeline stage caching (extends PL17).

schema.base_ttl_seconds: number — static TTL before dynamic adjustment
schema.dynamic_ttl_seconds: number|null — computed adaptive TTL (null if insufficient history)

creation_experience.description: Pipeline learning record capturing challenges, solutions, and quality outcomes for future reference. Enables learning loop across pipeline runs.

schema.schema_version: string — schema format version (default '1.0'). Readers MUST handle missing field as pre-13.2.0 format with null defaults.
schema.challenges: [{ stage: string, issue: string, resolution: string }] — problems encountered during pipeline
schema.quality_outcome: { weighted_score: number, grade: string, dimension_scores: { cognitive: number, intrinsic: number, detail: number } }
schema.simulation_highlights: { scenario_coverage: string, dead_nodes: number, red_issues: number }
schema.research_insights: [{ finding: string, applied: boolean }] — most impactful research findings

intent_vectors.description: Optional pre-computed semantic embedding vectors for NLU intent routing. Enables vector-similarity matching alongside keyword-based NLU classification.

schema.similarity_threshold: number — minimum cosine similarity for auto-routing (default: 0.85)

intent_vectors.note: Requires external embedding model. When unavailable, NLU falls back to keyword+LLM classification (existing behavior). This is an optional acceleration, not a requirement.

program_identity.description: Verifiable program identity for provenance and supply chain integrity.

schema.program_id: string — globally unique identifier using reverse-DNS format (e.g., 'aiap.dev/aiap_creator')
schema.signature: string|null — Ed25519 signature of governance_hash (optional, enables cryptographic verification)
schema.signature_public_key: string|null — Ed25519 public key for signature verification
schema.registry_url: string|null — URL in AIAP registry for discovery

program_identity.verification: I11 checks: (1) program_id format valid (reverse-DNS), (2) publisher is non-empty, (3) if signature present, verify against governance_hash using signature_public_key, (4) if registry_url present, verify URL format.
program_identity.relationship: Extends governance_hash (PL14) with active identity verification. governance_hash verifies content integrity; program_identity verifies publisher provenance.

elicitation.description: Standardized human-in-the-loop interaction format for Pipeline gates and decision points.

schema.context: string|null — additional context for informed decision-making
schema.options: [{id: string, label: string, description: string}]|null — for choice type only
schema.fields: [{name: string, type: string, required: boolean, default: any}]|null — for form type only
schema.timeout_seconds: number|null — auto-proceed after timeout with default_action
schema.default_action: string|null — action taken on timeout or user skip

schema.modified_changes: { change_id: { scope_override: string, rationale: string } } — user-modified changes with overrides
schema.dependency_acknowledged: boolean — user has reviewed dependency matrix
schema.user_notes: string|null — free-form user comments on evolution decisions

evolution_decision.defaults: All LEVEL_B changes approved, all LEVEL_C auto-applied.
evolution_decision.timeout_behavior: If no response within timeout_seconds, apply default_action (approve all LEVEL_B).

modes.form: Structured data collection with typed fields. Use for EvolveStep LEVEL_A/B change presentation where user provides per-item accept/reject decisions.
modes.choice: Selection from predefined options. Use for PostSimulateGate RED handling (auto-fix / proceed / abort) and QualityGate retry decisions.
modes.confirm: Binary yes/no approval. Use for AutoFixGate auto-fix approval and simple pipeline gate decisions.

elicitation.enforcement: PL19 defines which pipeline gates SHOULD use elicitation format.
elicitation.backward_compatibility: If executor does not support structured elicitation, fall back to natural language presentation (existing behavior). Elicitation is advisory — pipeline execution is never blocked by elicitation format support.

pipeline_task.description: Async pipeline task representation for progress tracking, cooperative cancellation, and optional external event emission.

schema.task_id: string — UUID v4, same as pipeline_run_id from pipeline_metadata
schema.status: string — pending | running | paused | blocked | completed | cancelled | failed
schema.estimated_remaining_seconds: number|null — computed from stage_timings historical averages
schema.cancel_requested: boolean — set to true to request cooperative cancellation
schema.effective_trust_level: number|null — runtime trust level (1-4). Defaults to AIAP.md trust_level.level at pipeline start. Executor MAY downgrade during execution (e.g., T4->T3). Gate stages (QualityGate, PostSimulateGate) MUST check: if effective_trust_level < declared level, apply stricter thresholds. Cannot increase above declared level.
schema.event_hooks: object|null — optional external event emission configuration (see event_hooks_schema)

event_hooks_schema.description: EVENT EMISSION (optional): pipeline_task MAY include event_hooks for external system integration. Pipelines without event_hooks configured operate identically to v13.0.0.

schema.on_gate_pass: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { gate: string, result: 'PASS', score: number, timestamp: ISO_8601 } }
schema.on_gate_fail: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { stage: string, reason: string, severity: string } }
schema.on_gate_warn: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { stage: string, details: string } }
schema.on_complete: { emit_target: 'webhook'|'stdout'|'file', payload_template: { version: string, quality_score: number, duration_seconds: number } }

event_hooks_schema.emission_points: QualityGate (PASS -> on_gate_pass, FAIL -> on_gate_fail), PostSimulateGate (GREEN -> on_gate_pass, YELLOW -> on_gate_warn, BLOCKED -> on_gate_fail), ReviewFinalize completion (on_complete).
event_hooks_schema.transport_note: Event transport implementations are executor-specific — STANDARD defines the schema, not the transport.

state_machine.transitions: pending→running (pipeline starts), running→paused (user pause), paused→running (user resume), running→blocked (STANDARD integrity BLOCK — core infrastructure missing), running→completed (pipeline success), running→cancelled (cancel_requested honored at stage boundary), running→failed (unrecoverable error)
state_machine.terminal_states: completed, cancelled, failed, blocked — no further transitions allowed
state_machine.trust_downgrade: effective_trust_level can decrease (never increase above declared) at any stage boundary. Immediate effect on subsequent stages. Record: pipeline_metadata.trust_downgrade_events = [{ from_level, to_level, at_stage, reason }].

pipeline_task.consumers: UI layers, monitoring dashboards, pipeline orchestrator, observability module, external systems (via event_hooks)

capability_negotiation.description: Runtime capability negotiation between AIAP programs. Programs declare offered and required capabilities; DependencyCheck computes negotiation results to determine interoperability.

schema.required: [string] — capabilities this program needs from dependencies (e.g., 'file_read')
schema.negotiation_result: string — 'full' (all required met) | 'partial' (some met, WARNING) | 'incompatible' (critical unmet, YELLOW)
schema.negotiated: [string] — intersection of offered and required that matched

self_validation.description: Pre-negotiation self-check: verify program's own capabilities.offered are supportable by its tools[] and capabilities.required are satisfiable by its tools[] or declared dependencies. Run before external dependency negotiation.

schema.offered_valid: boolean — true if all offered capabilities have supporting tools
schema.required_valid: boolean — true if all required capabilities have satisfying tools or dependencies
schema.unsupported_offered: [string] — offered capabilities with no supporting tool
schema.unsatisfied_required: [string] — required capabilities with no satisfying tool or dependency

capability_negotiation.consumed_by: Pipeline context (interoperability decisions), K5 simulation scenarios

sampling_request.description: LLM sampling protocol for modules that need additional LLM inference during pipeline execution. Controlled by PL23 with token budget, rate limiting, prompt sanitization, and audit trail.

schema.requesting_module: string — module id that initiated sampling
schema.prompt: string — sanitized prompt text sent to LLM
schema.max_tokens: number — maximum tokens, must be <= 20% of PL15 pipeline token_budget
schema.context: object|null — structured context passed to sampling

audit.prompt_sanitized: boolean — whether C2 guard was applied
audit.injection_detected: boolean — whether injection patterns found and neutralized
audit.budget_remaining_pct: number — remaining token budget percentage after this request
audit.request_sequence: number — 1-based sequence within pipeline run (max 5 per PL23)

schema.fallback_result: string|null — hardcoded decision result if sampling failed

sampling_request.produced_by: Any module invoking LLM sampling during pipeline execution

aiap_package.description: AIAP packaging format for distributing programs as self-contained .aiap archives (ZIP with manifest.json). Enables registry publishing, integrity verification, and governance hash validation.

schema.files: [{ path: string, sha256: string, size_bytes: number }] — all files with integrity hashes

ui_output.description: UI rendering instructions for AIAP program dashboards, forms, and visualizations.

schema.components: array — [{type, title, data_source, config}] UI component declarations
schema.interactivity: boolean — true if components accept user input (forms)

auto_fix_proposal.description: Automated fix proposal generated from ThreeDimTest/Simulation issues.

schema.proposals: [{ issue_id: string, severity: string (RED/YELLOW/ADVISORY), category: string (format/consistency/metadata/reference), file_path: string, current_content: string, proposed_content: string, impact_scope: [string], confidence: string (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), auto_applicable: boolean, status: string (pending/approved/applied/rejected) }]
schema.summary: { total: number, auto_applicable: number, manual_required: number, applied: number }
schema.snapshot_id: string — pre-fix file snapshot ID for rollback

lint_report.description: Automated verification results for objectively testable ThreeDimTest check items. Separates machine-verifiable results from AI subjective scores.

schema.automated_checks: [{ check_id: string (D2/D4/D6/I7/MF1/MF6/MF15/PL14/I13a/I13b), check_name: string, result: string (PASS/FAIL/WARNING), detail: string, evidence: string }]
schema.summary: { total_automated: number, passed: number, failed: number, coverage_percentage: number -- automated / total ThreeDimTest items }
schema.subjective_items: [string] -- items that remain AI-judged (e.g., C1, C3, C6, I5, D1, D5)

tool_dir_manifest.description: Tool directory manifest for Pattern G programs. Describes bundled tool implementations, MCP server configuration, file integrity hashes, and A2A agent card. Produced during GenerateToolDirs and verified by Code Trust Gate during pack/unpack.

schema.tool_dirs: [{ type: string (python_tools|ts_tools|go_tools|rust_tools|shell_tools|mcp_tools|a2a_tools|agentskills_tools|n8n_tools|other_tools), runtime: string, entry_point: string, file_count: number }]
schema.mcp_server: { schema_version: string, server_id: string, transport: 'stdio', runtime: string, entry_point: string, exposed_tools: [{ name: string, description: string }], file_hashes: { [filename]: sha256_hex }, governance: { aiap_protocol: string, trust_level: string, governance_hash: string } }
schema.agent_card: { name: string, description: string, capabilities: [string], endpoint: string } | null (optional, A2A v1.0)

tool_dir_manifest.consumed_by: review.aisop.json (I13/MF16 validation), simulate.aisop.json (Category M testing), advisor.aisop.json (package Code Trust Gate)

directory_layout.{version}/main.aisop.json: Copy of the AISOP file(s) at this version
directory_layout.{version}/quality_baseline.json: Quality scores and simulation coverage from this version's pipeline run

meta_schema.grades: { cognitive: number, intrinsic: number, detail: number }
meta_schema.pipeline_metadata: object|null — full pipeline_metadata from the pipeline run that produced this version

evolution_snapshot.description: Complete pre-evolution directory snapshot for rollback safety (P1.2). Created by PipelineStart before any file modifications begin during Evolve pipeline.
evolution_snapshot.contents: All .aisop.json files + AIAP.md + quality_baseline.json from target program directory

snapshot_meta_schema.file_manifest: [{ name: string, sha256: string }] — per-file integrity hashes

lifecycle.created_by: main.aisop.json PipelineStart (Evolve mode only, skipped for Create)
lifecycle.quality_gate: ReviewFinalize compares current weighted_score against snapshot quality_baseline.weighted_score per PL26
lifecycle.cleanup: Deleted after successful Finalize. Retained on pipeline failure for manual recovery.

simulation_categories.description: Standardized test scenario categories for comprehensive AISOP simulation. Categories F-L defined in AIAP_Standard.ecosystem.aisop.json.

categories.A_core_intents: Test each NLU intent with representative input. One scenario per intent minimum.
categories.B_boundary_rules: Test each NLU boundary rule with ambiguous input that could match multiple intents. One scenario per rule.
categories.C_multi_step_workflows: Test chains of operations requiring state persistence or sequential interaction (e.g., search→save→compare).
categories.D_error_paths: Test each Error fallback by simulating tool failures (search fails, file read fails, write fails).
categories.E_edge_cases: Test input limits (>max length, empty, injection, path traversal, mixed language, emoji), output limits (>max items), and guard triggers. SUB-CATEGORIES: see AIAP_Standard.performance.aisop.json for E.figurative and E.injection sub-category definitions.

simulation_categories.state_transition_applicability: Category I applies only to stateful patterns (Pattern E, or any pattern with file_system write side_effects). For stateless patterns, Category I scenario count is 0 and does not affect coverage formula.
simulation_categories.trust_boundary_applicability: Category J applies to programs with trust_level >= T3 (supervised or autonomous). For T1/T2 programs, Category J scenario count is 0 and does not affect coverage formula.
simulation_categories.multi_agent_applicability: Category K applies to programs with ecosystem_map in AIAP.md OR non-empty dependencies[] in AIAP.md. Standalone programs without ecosystem or dependency declarations: Category K scenario count is 0, does not affect coverage formula.

domain_scenario_template.description: Domain-specific simulation scenario templates that supplement standard A-O categories.

schema.domain: string — domain keyword (companion_ai, code_assistant, customer_service, etc.)
schema.scenarios: [{ id, category, description, trigger, expected_behavior, severity }]
schema.minimum_coverage: number — minimum scenarios required for domain compliance

built_in_domains.companion_ai: emotional manipulation resistance, long-term personality consistency, cross-cultural sensitivity, crisis protocol resilience, boundary pushing detection, dependency detection
built_in_domains.code_assistant: code injection prevention, license compliance, unsafe code detection, multi-language consistency
built_in_domains.customer_service: escalation protocol, SLA compliance, PII handling, complaint resolution

domain_scenario_template.loading: simulate.aisop.json reads AIAP.md domain_tags or summary to auto-detect domain, loads matching template, generates domain scenarios as Category P (Domain-Specific Behavioral)
domain_scenario_template.custom: Programs may provide custom_scenarios.json in program directory for additional domain scenarios

evolution_rules.description: EvolveFlow uses LEVEL_A/B/C classification instead of ratio-based limits. Separates structural evolution from quality improvement.

LEVEL_A.name: 用户决定 (Structural)
LEVEL_A.scope: Pattern upgrade/downgrade, module add/remove, tool set changes, statefulness changes (stateless→session→persistent).
LEVEL_A.decision: MUST have explicit user confirmation. Never auto-apply.

LEVEL_B.name: 引擎建议 (Functional)
LEVEL_B.scope: New function nodes, NLU intent add/remove, cross-module data flow changes, high-impact enhancements.
LEVEL_B.decision: Engine recommends with importance score (1-5). User can accept/reject/override.

LEVEL_C.name: 引擎自决 (Incremental)
LEVEL_C.scope: New steps within existing functions, boundary rule improvements, search strategy optimization, formatting.
LEVEL_C.decision: Auto-apply. User notified but no confirmation required.

constraints.max_changes_per_evolution: No hard cap. Recommend <=15 for manageability. CAP SCOPE RULE: EVOLVE CAP counts LEVEL_A + LEVEL_B changes only — these introduce new capabilities with higher complexity and risk. LEVEL_C improvements (version fixes, error field additions, algorithm specifications) are NOT counted toward CAP — they are low-risk incremental improvements. ModifyStep fixes (signal path corrections, variable initialization, consumer documentation) are tracked separately as modifystep_fix_count and NEVER counted toward CAP. REPORTING: EvolveStep MUST display: 'Feature changes (A+B): {count}/{cap} CAP | Improvements (C): {count} (not counted) | ModifyStep fixes: tracked separately'.
constraints.separation: EvolveFlow handles structural/functional changes only. Quality improvements (I2 guards, I4 circuit-breakers, D4 naming) belong to ModifyFlow.

version_management.description: Unified version bump rules for all pipeline operations.

rules.modify_only: patch+1 — quality fixes without functional changes
rules.evolve_level_b: minor+1 — functional changes without structural changes
rules.evolve_level_a: major+1 — structural changes (pattern upgrade, module add/remove, tool set changes)
rules.mixed: highest applicable level wins (A > B > C)
rules.reset_rule: Per semantic versioning: minor+1 resets patch to 0 (e.g., 1.0.2 → 1.1.0 not 1.1.2); major+1 resets minor and patch to 0 (e.g., 1.2.3 → 2.0.0 not 2.2.3)

version_management.enforcement: Generate ApplyFixes and main ReviewPresent/ReviewFinalize MUST reference these rules for version computation. No inline version logic — always delegate to STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management.

cascade_on_reject.description: Dependency cascade when LEVEL_A items are rejected by user during EvolveStep (P2.5).
cascade_on_reject.trigger: User rejects one or more LEVEL_A items in EvolveStep interactive confirmation.

algorithm.step1: For each rejected LEVEL_A item, traverse evolution_plan.dependency_matrix to find all LEVEL_B and LEVEL_C items that reference the rejected item as a dependency.
algorithm.step2_cascade: Items whose ONLY dependencies are rejected items → auto-reject (cascade). These items cannot function without their sole upstream dependency.
algorithm.step2_degrade: Items with MIXED dependencies (some rejected, some accepted) → mark as DEGRADED. The item proceeds but uses hardcoded defaults or STANDARD-defined defaults instead of the rejected item's configuration.
algorithm.step3_notify: Present cascade summary to user: 'Rejected: {A_list}. Cascaded (auto-rejected): {cascade_list}. Degraded (using defaults): {degrade_list}.' User may override individual cascade/degrade decisions.
algorithm.step4_record: Record cascade_results in pipeline_metadata: { rejected_a_items: [], cascaded_items: [], degraded_items: [{ id, missing_dependency, fallback_source }] }

degraded_mode.definition: A LEVEL_B/C item operating without its intended LEVEL_A configuration dependency. Functions with reduced capability using default values.
degraded_mode.marking: evolution_plan items in degraded mode carry: { degraded: true, missing_dependency: string, fallback_source: 'standard_default' | 'hardcoded' }
degraded_mode.generate_enforcement: generate.aisop.json EvolutionVerify MUST detect degraded items and verify hardcoded defaults are used. Dangling references to rejected configurations trigger DEGRADED_REFERENCE_ERROR with auto-substitution of default values.

generation_hygiene.description: Standards for content quality and meaningfulness in generated AISOP files. Nihil density analysis ensures generated content is substantive, not filler.

nihil_density.definition: Nihil density = ratio of meaningless content characters to total content characters in a generated AISOP file.

categories.FILLER: Generic phrases adding no specification value (e.g., 'as needed', 'when appropriate').
categories.REDUNDANCY: Identical or near-identical text repeated within or across modules beyond protocol-mandated patterns.
categories.DEAD_LOGIC: Unreachable code branches, orphan nodes, conditions that always evaluate to same value.
categories.VACUOUS_CONSTRAINT: Constraints imposing no verifiable restriction (e.g., 'must be valid', 'should work correctly').
categories.PLACEHOLDER: TODO/TBD/FIXME markers, template text not customized for the target domain.
categories.TAUTOLOGY: Conditions restating their own definition without adding information.
categories.INFLATION: Unnecessarily verbose descriptions compressible >50% without information loss.

thresholds.critical: nihil_ratio >= 0.25 — significant meaningless content, cleanup strongly advised

exemptions: Error field templates (intentionally standardized per protocol)
nihil_density.enforcement: Advisory — nihil density does not block pipeline. WARNING/CRITICAL status reported in ReviewPresent. .nihil_backup/ created for rollback capability.

pipeline_integration.stage: NihilDensityStep — positioned between Research3 (compliance) and ValidateStep in the pipeline.
pipeline_integration.rationale: Post-compliance, pre-validation placement ensures generated content is analyzed for quality after all generation and research stages complete, and before formal validation begins.
pipeline_integration.data_flow: NihilDensityStep produces nihil_density_report → consumed by ValidateStep (context), ReviewPresent (display), ReviewFinalize (persistence to quality_baseline).

aisop.main:
graph TD
  Start[Start Node] --> Check{Decision?}
  Check --> |Yes| SubTask[Call Sub-Graph]
  Check --> |No| AtomicTool[Call Function]
  SubTask --> endNode((End))
  AtomicTool --> endNode

Start.step1: Parse input. Validate: type guard, injection guard, path guard, size guard, encoding guard.
Start.Error: Invalid -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: show input format guide -> inform.

Check.step1: Classify input into intent A or intent B. CONFIDENCE: <0.80 -> present both options -> ask user.
Check.constraints: Boundary rule: intent A when X present; intent B when Y present.
Check.Error: Unclassifiable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present binary choice -> inform.

AtomicTool.step1: Execute tool action. Atomic write: .tmp rename + .bak backup.
AtomicTool.Error: Fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display result as inline text -> inform.

SubTask.Error: Sub-graph execution fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: inline sub-graph steps as direct execution -> inform.
